method.instruction: Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add turmeric powder, mangoes and salt. Mix. Add water. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Place cooker on high heat. Add sugar. Stir till sugar dissolves and chutney thickens (approximately 3 minutes).
Place chutney on serving dish. Serve at room temperature or cool.

name: Aloo Gobi ki Tehari Rice with Potatoes and Cauliflower Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi

method.instruction: Grind together ginger and garlic into a paste.

Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add onions and fry till golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and stir for a few seconds. Add tomatoes, turmeric, chilli, coriander and cumin powders. Cook on medium heat till oil shows separately (approximately 5 minutes), stirring occasionally. Add all other ingredients except water and coriander leaves. Stir and fry for 2 minutes. Add water. Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight.
Open cooker. Serve hot, garnished with coriander leaves.

name: Aloo Talasani
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi (Saucepan)

method.instruction: Grind onion and ginger into a paste.
Grind cloves and cinnamon into a powder.
Put 1 cup water and potatoes in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook 1 minute.
Remove cooker from heat.  Press finger-tip control/slightly lift vent weight to lightly release pressure.
Open cooker. Remove and peel potatoes. Sprinkle ¾ tsp turmeric powder on potatoes. Mix.
Group potatoes into 3 batches. Heat oil in a deep-frying pan for about 5 minutes. Fry each batch till golden brown and remove. Strain oil.

In a large pan, heat ¼ cup (strained) oil for about 2 minutes. Add bay leaves and cumin seeds. Stir for a few seconds. Add chopped onion and fry till golden brown. Add ground paste. Stir fry for about 2 minutes.
Add tomato, chilli, cumin and coriander powders, salt, sugar and remaining turmeric (1 tsp). Cook till oil shows separately (approximately 3 minutes), stirring occasionally.

Add potatoes. Stir gently to coat potatoes with masala. Add powdered spices and remaining water (1 cup). Stir. Reduce heat to low and simmer till potatoes are hot (approximately 5 minutes). Once during cooking, move potatoes around gently.
Pour ghee evenly over potatoes. Serve hot.

name: Aloor Potoler Rasa
translation: Potato and Parwal Curry - Orissa Style

method.instruction: Grind cloves, cardamoms and 3 sticks cinnamon into a powder.

Heat vanaspati in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add whole chillies and cumin seeds. Stir for a few seconds. Add remaining cinnamon (2 sticks), onions and fry till onions are golden brown. Add powdered spices and all other ingredients except water. Stir fry for about 3 minutes. Add water. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Serve hot.

name: Alu Tikki
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Flat Tava (Griddle)

name: Alu Tikki
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm

method.instruction: Immerse each bread slice in water for 15 seconds. Squeeze out and discard water. Break bread into tiny pieces. Mix bread and all other ingredients except oil. Make 12 patties about 2¾ inch/7 cm in diameter, 1/2 inch/1.3 cm thick.
Heat frying pan on medium heat about 2 minutes. Add 1 tbsp/ 15 ml oil and heat about 30 seconds. Spread oil in base of pan.
Add 4 patties. Fry on both sides till golden brown (about 2 minutes on each side). Remove.
Fry remaining patties in the same way as in step 3, adding and immediately spreading 1 tbsp/15 ml oil in pan for each batch. Serve hot, accompanied with chutney or tomato ketchup.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm Frying Pan,  Step 2: Heat pan about 1½ minutes. Heat 2 ¼  tsp/11.3 ml oil about 30 seconds. Each batch: 2¼  tsp/11.3 ml oil; 3 tikki.
For 22 cm Frying Pan (Rounded Sides): Step 2: Heat pan about 2 minutes. Heat 2 ¼  tsp/11.3 ml oil about 30 seconds. Each batch: 2 ¼  tsp/11.3 ml oil; 3 tikki.
For 30 cm Frying Pan, Step 2: Heat pan about 2½ minutes. Heat 1 tbsp + ¾ tsp/19 ml oil about 30 seconds. Each batch: 1 tbsp + ¾  tsp/19 ml oill; 5 tikki.

name: Alu Tikki
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Flat Tava 30 cm

name: Alu Tikki
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: Immerse each bread slice in water for 15 seconds.  Squeeze out and discard water.  Break bread into tiny pieces.  Mix bread and all other ingredients except oil.  Make 12 patties about 2¾ inch/7 cm in diameter, ½ inch/1.3 cm thick.
Heat 1½ tsp/7.5 ml oil in frying pan on medium heat about 3 minutes (for pan other than 26 cm see

special_instructions.instruction: below).  Add 4 patties.  Fry on both sides till golden brown (about 3 minutes on each side).  Remove.  Fry remaining patties in the same way.  Serve hot, accompanied with chutney or tomato ketchup.
For 18 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Step 2: Heat ¾ tsp/3.8 ml oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.  Add 2 patties.
For 22 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Step 2: Heat 1 1/8 tsp/5.6 ml oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.  Add 3 patties.
For 30 cm pan, in Step 2, heat 2¼ tsp oil in pan on medium heat about 3 minutes.  Add 6 patties.

method.instruction: In a pan, roast together Bengal gram and cumin seeds till gram is golden brown. Remove and keep aside.
In the same pan, separately roast chillies and remove.
Grind roasted ingredients with coconut into a powder.
Pour water into cooker. Add bananas.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight. Open cooker.
Transfer bananas to a colander and drain.

In a pan, heat oil for about 3 minutes. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add curry leaves and black gram. Fry till gram is golden brown. Add bananas, ground spices and all other ingredients. Stir and fry for about 3 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Avial
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi (Saucepan)

method.instruction: Grind into a paste coconut, green chillies and cumin seeds, adding ½ cup water gradually.
Put remaining water (½ cup), yam, cluster beans and snake gourd in Handi. Bring to boil on high heat.
Reduce heat to low. Cover. Put ¼ cup water on lid. Simmer for 10 minutes, or till vegetables are half cooked.
Add French beans, banana, drumstick, pumpkin, salt and turmeric powder. Mix. Cover. Add ¼ cup water to water already on lid. Simmer till vegetables are tender (about 20 minutes).
Add coconut paste, curd, tomato and curry leaves. Mix. Increase heat to high. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low. Cover. Add ¼ cup water to water already on lid. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat. Pour coconut oil over avial. Serve hot with rice.

name: Avial
translation: Vegetable in Coconut and Curd - Kerala Style

method.instruction: Grind ginger and 1 tbsp cumin seeds into a paste, adding a little water (2 tbsp) from time to time.
Grind cardamoms, cloves and cinnamon into a paste, adding remaining water (1 tbsp) from time to time.
Heat oil in cooker for about 5 minutes. Add bay leaves, ginger paste and remaining cumin seeds (½ tsp). Stir for a few seconds. Add cabbage, potatoes and peas. Stir fry for about 2 minutes.
Add all other ingredients except spice-paste, milk, flour and ghee. Mix
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker.
In a bowl, gradually add milk to flour, blending until smooth. Stir into cooker.
Place cooker on high heat. Cook for about 2 minutes stirring gently.
Remove cooker from heat. Add spice-paste. Stir. Pour ghee over kopi. Serve hot.

name: Batata Wadas
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

name: Bhuni Chana Dal aur Paneer
translation: Fried  Bengal Gram with Cottage Cheese

method.instruction: To make paneer: Bring milk to boil in a pan. Allow to cool. Skim off all fat from the surface. Bring milk to boil again. Gradually add citric acid, stirring constantly. When milk curdles, remove pan from heat and leave covered for 15 minutes. Place muslin over a bowl. Pour curdled milk into muslin and strain. Gather up corners of muslin and tie about 5 cm above curds. Hang this bag for about 15 minutes. Gently squeeze bag, take down and place between two cutting boards. Place on top board, about 2 kg heavy object for about 1 hour. Remove weight. Untie bag and remove paneer.
Grind ginger and garlic into a paste.
Group paneer into 3 batches. Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Lightly brown each batch and remove. Drain off excess oil leaving 2/3 cup in cooker.
Place cooker on high heat. Add whole chillies, cloves, bay leaves, cinnamon and cardamoms. Stir for a few seconds. Add onions and fry till golden brown.

Add ginger-garlic paste. Stir for a few seconds. Add turmeric and chilli powders, tomatoes and ¼ cup water. Cook till tomatoes are pulpy and oil shows separately ( approximately 5 minutes), stirring occasionally.
Add Bengal gram and salt. Stir fry for about 3 minutes. Add remaining water ( 4 cups). Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 4 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Place cooker on medium heat. Add paneer. Simmer for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve hot, garnished with coriander leaves.

name: Breakfast Tomatoes Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm
Breakfast Tomatoes Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm

method.instruction: Melt butter in frying pan on medium heat. As soon as butter starts browning, add tomatoes (cut side down). Fry on both sides till slightly browned (about 11/2 minutes on each side).
Place tomatoes (cut side up) on serving dish. Pour leftover pan juices on tomatoes. Sprinkle salt and pepper. Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm Frying Pan: No change required
For 22 cm (Rounded Sides): No change required.
For 30 cm Frying Pan: Increase ingredients by one-half.

name: Cabbage and Peas - Bengal Style Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan
Cabbage in Milk Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre

method.instruction: Bring milk to boil in the Bowl on medium-high heat, stirring occasionally.  Gradually add cabbage. Stir. Boil for 2 minutes. Add salt and pepper. Stir. Remove from heat.

In a separate pan, melt butter on low heat. Sprinkle in flour and cook, stirring constantly with a wire whisk for about 2 minutes. Remove from heat. Take ½ cup milk from the Bowl and stirring constantly with whisk, gradually add to butter-flour mixture. Mix. Add to cabbage, stirring constantly.
Boil on medium-high heat, stirring constantly, till milk thickens (about 2 minutes). Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: When using the 2 Litre Bowl, cook on low or medium heat only.  Cooking on high heat may damage the plastic handles.  For 2 Litre Bowl, reduce the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%).  As this is a smaller vessel than the 3 Litre Bowl, this recipe may cook about 20% faster than the times stated.  Please check after 80% of the stated cooking time to see whether the food is cooked as you would like it and adjust the cooking time as required.
When using the 4 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%) and increase cooking time as required.
When using the 5 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by two-thirds (66%) and increase cooking time as required.
When using the 6 Litre Bowl, you may double the quantity of ingredients (200%) and increase cooking time as required.

name: Cabbage Rolls Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre and Futura Hard Anodised Stewpot 3 Litre

method.instruction: Put all ingredients except yogurt in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 4 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Discard bay leaf. Mix the soup smooth in a blender or mash it through a sieve.
Return soup to cooker. Reheat. Serve hot. Garnish each individual serving with a tablespoon of yogurt.

special_instructions.instruction: To cook reduced quantities: reduce all ingredients proportionately.

method.instruction: Heat oil in cooker for about 5 minutes. Add mustard seeds, red chillies, aniseed, fenugreek, onion and cumin seeds. When mustard seeds crackle, add green chillies, radish and potatoes. Stir and fry for about 2 minutes. Add beans, pumpkin and brinjals. Stir and fry for about 8 minutes. Add spinach and all other ingredients except water.
Mix well, pushing down spinach. Sprinkle water.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight. Open cooker.
Serve hot.
To cook reduced quantities : do not reduce water at all.
Combine orange and lemon peels, dates, pecans, currants, raisins, brandy and lemon juice. Mix well.
Sift together flour, cinnamon, cloves and nutmeg. Add bread crumbs. Stir.
Mix butter and sugar. Beat till light and fluffy. Gradually add eggs, beating well after each addition. Fold in prepared flour and fruit and nut mixture.
Spoon the mixture into a greased 1 quart (1 litre) mold. Cover mold tightly with aluminum foil securely tied.
Pour water into cooker. Put grid in cooker. Place mold on grid.
Close cooker without pressure regulator. Cook on high heat until steam passes through steam vent. Reduce heat and cook for 15 minutes, ensuring a steady stream of steam from steam vent.
Place pressure regulator in position. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 50 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.

Open cooker. Take out and uncover mold. Run a knife around edge of mold. Place a serving dish on top of mold and invert. Shake gently to release. Remove mold. Serve pudding hot, accompanied with hard sauce. For Hard Sauce:
Combine butter and sugar. Beat until creamy. Add brandy. Continue beating until well blended.
Add nutmeg. Chill until hard (approximately 1 hour).

name: Coconut Chutney on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava

method.instruction: Put groundnuts/peanuts on tava on medium heat. Stir constantly till groundnuts start crackling (about 5 minutes). Remove from tava. Allow to cool. Remove skins by rubbing groundnuts.
Grind into a paste groundnuts, coconut, curd, chillies, salt and sugar, gradually adding water

Tempering: Heat oil in a small pan on medium heat about 1 minute. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add urad dal. Stir a few seconds. Add curry leaves. Stir constantly till dal is light golden brown. Immediately pour over chutney. Mix. Serve with Traditional South Indian Dosa, Crispy Dosa, Masala Dosa, Rawa Dosa, Uttapam and Three-at-One-Time Masala Uttapam.

name: Coconut Chutney Futura Nonstick Flat Tava 30 cm
Dalma
translation: Green Gram with Vegetables - Orissa Style

method.instruction: In a pan, roast green gram on medium heat till light brown (approximately 8 minutes), stirring constantly. Remove and wash roasted gram.
Put gram, water, turmeric powder and salt in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Reduce pressure by placing cooker in a basin of cold water for about 2 minutes.
Open cooker. Add all other ingredients except those for tempering. Mix.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker.

For tempering, heat vanaspati in a pan for about 2 minutes. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add bay leaves, cumin seeds and chillies. Stir for a few seconds. Add onion and fry till golden brown. Add tomato. Cook till tomato is pulpy and vanaspati shows separately (approximately 2 minutes), stirring occasionally. Pour over dalma. Stir. Serve hot.

name: Dhania Chutney
cookware_note: on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava

method.instruction: In a pan, roast together brown cardamoms, cloves, cumin and poppy seeds. Grind roasted ingredients together with onion, ginger and garlic into a paste, adding a little water (¼ cup) from time to time.
Put 1 cup water and potatoes in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Remove Potatoes. Peel and prick each potato all over with a fork. Wash and wipe dry cooker.
Group potatoes into 3 batches. Heat oil in a deep-frying pan for about 5 minutes. Fry each batch till golden brown and remove. Strain oil and put 7 tbsp into cooker.

Place cooker on high heat. Add whole chillies, cinnamon and bay leaves. Stir for a few seconds. Add grated onions and fry till golden brown. Add ground paste, tomato puree, salt, chilli and turmeric powders. Stir for a few seconds.

Add 1 tbsp curd. Stir fry until curd is well blended (approximately ½ minute). Add remaining curd in the same way, 1 tbsp at a time till all curd is used. Stir fry till oil shows separately (approximately 3 minutes). Add potatoes and stir gently to coat the potatoes with masala. Add remaining water (2 ½cups). Simmer on low heat for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add garam masala powder. Stir. Serve hot.

name: Dum Aloo
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi (Saucepan)

method.instruction: To cook reduced quantities : do not reduce water at all.
Put water and beets in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 16 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Drain, peel and cut beets into 1 inch (2.5 cm) cubes.

In a large pan, melt butter on medium heat. Sprinkle in flour and cook on low heat, stirring constantly with a wire whisk, for about 3 minutes. Remove pan from heat. Still using whisk, gradually add hot water. Return cooker to medium heat. Cook, stirring constantly, till sauce thickens (approximately 5 minutes).
Add beets and all other ingredients. Stir.
Simmer on low heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Serve hot.

name: Erissery
translation: Pumpkin and Red Karamani Beans with Coconut

name: French Beans with Coconut and Garlic Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi

method.instruction: Finely grind coconut, green chillies and garlic.
Heat oil in Handi on medium heat for about 3 minutes. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add curry leaves. Stir for a few seconds. Add beans, salt and turmeric powder. Mix.
Reduce heat to low. Cover. Put ¼ cup water on lid. Cook till beans are half tender (about 10 minutes).
Add coconut mixture. Mix. Cover. Add ¼ cup water to water already on lid. Cook till beans are tender (about 10 minutes). Serve hot with chapatti.

name: French Fries Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Melt 5 tablespoons butter in cooker. Add all other ingredients except water and flour. Stir well. Add water.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 1 minute. Remove cooker from heat. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight.
Open cooker.
In a bowl, mix together flour and remaining butter (2 tablespoons). Tilt cooker and add the mixture to liquid in cooker. Move cooker in circular motion to mix contents well.
Place cooker on medium heat. Cook for about 2 minutes, without stirring. Remove parsley. Serve hot.

name: Fresh Peas and Carrots with Lettuce

method.instruction: Put carrots and milk into cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure. Open cooker.

Place open cooker with carrots and milk on high heat. Add sugar. Cook till liquid dries up (approximately 15 minutes), stirring occasionally. Add mava and ghee. Cook till ghee shows separately (approximately 10 minutes), stirring constantly. Serve hot, garnished with almonds.

name: Green Beans and Tomatoes with Basil

method.instruction: Pour water into cooker. Add guavas.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 15 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Mash fruit to extract maximum juice. Put contents in a muslin. Gather up corners of muslin and tie about 5 cms. above fruit. Hang the bag to strain juice without squeezing.

Measure 4 cups of strained juice and pour into a large saucepan. Add lemon juice. Bring to boil on high heat. Reduce to medium heat. Add sugar gradually, stirring constantly until sugar is completely dissolved: Boil rapidly on high heat until setting point is reached.
Remove from heat. Skim off scum. Pour jelly immediately into hot sterile jars. Seal.

name: Hash Brown Potatoes Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm

method.instruction: Mix potatoes, onion, salt, pepper and lemon juice.

Heat frying pan on medium-high heat about 1 minute. Add butter and spread till melted. Add potato mixture. Mix. Reduce heat to mediumlow. Cover and cook 10 minutes. Uncover. Cook till potatoes are tender and light golden brown (about 15 minutes), stirring and scraping pan with a spatula. Partially mash potatoes. Pat to form a potato cake about 6 inch/15 cm in diameter, ¾ inch/1.9 cm thick. Fry till underside is browned and a crust has formed (about 5 minutes). Using a spatula, divide cake into 4 portions. Turn over each portion, reforming into round shape. Fry till browned and crust has formed (about 5 minutes). Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm Frying Pan: Reduce ingredients by one-third. Form potato cake about 4¾ inch/12 cm in diameter, ¾ inch/1.9 cm thick.
For 30 cm Frying Pan,Increase ingredients by two-thirds.Step 2: Heat pan about 2 minutes.Form potato cake about 8 inch/20 cm in diameter,¾ inch/1.9 cm thick.

name: Hash Brown Potatoes Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: Extract 1 ½ cups milk from coconut using 1 ½ cups water.
Grind together onions, aniseed, chillies and coriander leaves into a paste.
In a pan, roast together cloves, cinnamon, cardamoms and grind into a powder.

Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add sliced onions and fry till golden brown. Add ground paste. Stir fry for about 2 minutes. Add potatoes, peas, carrots, beans and cauliflower. Stir fry for about 2 minutes. Add powdered spices and salt. Stir for a few seconds. Add remaining water (2 cups). Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker.
Place cooker with vegetables on medium heat. Add coconut milk and bring to boil, stirring carefully.
Remove cooker from heat. Stir in lemon juice. Serve hot.
Grind coconut, green chillies, mustard and cumin seeds into a paste, adding a little water (¾ cup) from time to time.
Put remaining water (1 cup), yam, bananas, turmeric powder and salt in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Add ground paste, curd, curry leaves and pepper. Mix.
Place cooker on high heat. Cook till kalan is thickened (approximately 5 minutes), stirring occasionally.
For Tempering, heat oil in a pan for about 1 minute. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add chillies, fenugreek seeds and curry leaves. Stir for a few seconds and pour over kalan. Stir. Serve hot.

Heat oil in a pan for about ½ minute. Roast together coriander seeds, chillies, peppercorns and black gram till gram is golden brown. Remove and grind into a paste, adding a little water (1/3 cup) from time to time.
Put remaining water (4 cups), Bengal gram, turmeric powder and salt in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Add ground paste and all other ingredients except those for tempering. Mix.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Allow to cool naturally for 3 minutes. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker.

For tempering, heat oil in a pan for about 1 minute. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add chillies, curry leaves and coconut. Stir fry till coconut is golden brown and pour over kuttu curry. Stir. Serve hot.

name: Lemon Pudding with Orange Marmalade Sauce

method.instruction: To cook reduced quantities: in step 7, do not reduce water (½ cup) at all.
In a Bowl, add 4 cups water with 2 tsp salt. Put cauliflower in bowl and keep about 30 minutes. Drain.
Grind together poppy seeds and cashew nuts into a paste, adding a little water (3 tbsp) from time to time.
Separately grind together ginger, garlic and green chillies into a paste.
Heat oil in cooker about 2 minutes. Add onions and fry till golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste, tomatoes, turmeric, coriander, cumin and red chilli powders and remaining salt (2 ½ tsp). Cook till tomatoes are pulpy, stirring occasionally.

Add 1 tbsp curd. Stir fry till curd is well blended (approximately 30 seconds). Add remaining curd in the same way, a tablespoon at a time till all curd is used. Fry till oil shows separately (about 3 minutes).
Add cauliflower. Stir fry about 5 minutes. Add remaining water (½ cup). Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Add cashew nut paste. Return cooker to heat. Cook about 2 minutes,stirring gently. Serve hot, garnished with coriander leaves.

To make paneer: In a pan bring milk to boil, stirring occasionally to prevent skin from forming on top. Add lemon juice and stir till milk curdles (curd separates from whey). Remove pan from heat. Place muslin over bowl. Pour curdled milk into muslin and strain. Reserve 2 ¾ cups whey. Gather up corners of muslin and tie about 5 cm above curd. Hang this bag for about 30 minutes. Gently squeeze bag, take down and place between two cutting boards. Place on top board about 2 ¼ kg heavy object (saucepan filled with water) for about 2 hours until paneer layer is 1 cm thick. Remove weight. Untie bag and remove. Cut paneer into 2 ½ cm cubes.
Grind ginger and garlic into a paste.
Group paneer into 3 batches. Heat oil in cooker for about 3 minutes. Lightly brown each batch and remove. Drain off excess oil leaving ½ cup in cooker.
Place cooker on high heat. Add onions and fry till golden brown.

Add ginger-garlic paste. Stir for a few seconds. Add turmeric, chilli, coriander and cumin powders, salt, tomatoes and ¼ cup whey or water. Cook till tomatoes are pulpy and oil shows separately (approximately 5 minutes), stirring occasionally.
Add peas. Sir fry for about 2 minutes. Add remaining whey or water ( 2 ½ cups).
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 1 minute.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Place cooker on medium heat. Add paneer and simmer for about 5 minutes. Add garam masala powder. Stir.
Serve hot, garnished with coriander leaves.
Heat oil in cooker about 1 minute.  Add onion and garlic.  Stir fry till onion is transparent.  Add beans and stock.  Stir.
Close cooker.  Bring to full pressure on high heat.  Reduce heat and cook 5 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat.  Release pressure by placing cooker in about 4 inches/10 cm of cold water in a basin or in a sink for 2 minutes.
Open cooker.  Place cooker on high heat.  Bring to boil.  Add remaining ingredients except parsley and cheese.  Stir.
Close cooker.  Bring to full pressure on high heat.  Reduce heat and cook 6 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat.  Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker.  Add parsley.  Serve hot, garnished with cheese.
To cook reduced quantities: reduce all ingredients proportionately.

To prepare spices : Place cinnamon, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, anise seeds and fenugreek seeds in a small, heavy skillet on medium heat. Stir continuously (approximately 3 minutes) until the spices darken by a few shades and give out a stronger aroma. Grind roasted spices in a pestle and mortar or in the container of an electric coffee grinder.

To make coconut milk : Break open coconut by holding the coconut in one hand against a hard surface and hitting the coconut with the blunt side of a heavy cleaver all around its 'equator'. At the first sign of a crack, hold the coconut over a bowl to collect coconut water. Though not coconut milk, it makes a refreshing drink. Break the halves into smaller pieces. Slip a knife between coconut meat and the shell and pry loose the meat. Remove brown skin from the meat with a paring knife or potato peeler. Cut the coconut meat into cubes of about ½ inch (1 cm). There should be about 1 1/3 cups meat. (Minimum required is 1 cup.) Put coconut and 1/3 cup hot water in a blender and mix the mixture for about 2 minutes. Add another 1/3 cup hot water and stir. Blend for approximately 3 minutes until finely ground. Place muslin or a double thickness of cheesecloth over a bowl. Put mixture into cheesecloth, gather up the corners and squeeze through as much milk as possible. Measure ¾ cup milk and keep aside.
Heat oil in cooker for about 1 minute. Add carrot, turnip, onion, garlic and ginger. Stir fry for about 2 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients except coconut milk, rice and lemon juice. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Discard curry leaves. Mix the soup smooth in a blender or mash it through a sieve.
Return soup to cooker. Add coconut milk, rice and lemon juice. Stir. Reheat. Serve hot.

name: Mushroom Stew Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre

method.instruction: Heat oil and 1 tbsp butter in a pan on medium-high heat for about 1 minute. Add bay leaves, garlic and onion. Fry till onions are soft and just start to change colour. Reduce heat to low.

Sprinkle in flour and cook, stirring constantly for about 1 minute. Add pureed tomatoes and water. Mix. Add quartered tomatoes. Stir. Increase heat to medium-high. Bring to boil. Remove from heat and keep aside.
In the Bowl, melt remaining butter (1 ½ tbsp) on medium-high heat. Add thyme and mushrooms. Stir fry for about 2 minutes.
Add tomato mixture and white onions. Mix. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low. Simmer till onions are cooked, stirring occasionally (about 25 minutes).
Add salt, pepper, parsley and wine. Mix. Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: When using the 2 Litre Bowl, cook on low or medium heat only.  Cooking on high heat may damage the plastic handles.  For 2 Litre Bowl, reduce the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%).  As this is a smaller vessel than the 3 Litre Bowl, this recipe may cook about 20% faster than the times stated.  Please check after 80% of the stated cooking time to see whether the food is cooked as you would like it and adjust the cooking time as required.
When using the 4 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%) and increase cooking time as required.
When using the 5 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by two-thirds (66%) and increase cooking time as required.
When using the 6 Litre Bowl, you may double the quantity of ingredients (200%) and increase cooking time as required.

name: Navratan Khorma
translation: Mixed Vegetables and Cottage Cheese in Curd

name: Nine-at-One-Time Alu Tikki
cookware_note: on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava

method.instruction: Immerse each bread slice in water for 15 seconds. Squeeze out and discard water. Break bread into tiny pieces. Mix bread and all other ingredients except oil. Make 18 patties about 2¾ inch/7 cm in diameter, ½ inch/1.3 cm thick.
Heat 1 tbsp + 1 tsp/20 ml oil on centre of tava on medium heat no more than 5 minutes. Spread oil with spatula over tava to about 1 inch/2.5 cm from rim.
Add 9 patties. Fry on both sides till golden brown (about 5 minutes on each side). Remove.
Spread remaining oil (1 tbsp + 1 tsp/20 ml) on tava. Fry remaining patties in the same way as in step 3. Serve hot, accompanied with tomato ketchup or Dhania Chutney.
In a bowl, put beans, kand, brinjals, potatoes and bananas. Add 1 teaspoon turmeric powder, 2 teaspoons chilli powder and 2 teaspoons salt. Mix and keep aside.

Mix together coriander leaves, coconut, asafoetida, coriander powder, 4 teaspoons Bengal gram flour, 2 tablespoons sugar, ¼ teaspoon sodium bicarbonate, 1 teaspoon turmeric powder, 3 teaspoons salt and remaining chilli powder (3 tablespoons) to make the stuffing.
Fill each brinjal, potato and banana with 2 teaspoons of prepared stuffing.
Mix remaining stuffing with kand and beans.

Mix together fenugreek leaves, wheat flour, green chillies and remaining turmeric powder (½ teaspoon), salt (2 teaspoons), Bengal gram flour (¾ cup), sugar (2 tablespoons) and sodium bicarbonate (½ teaspoon). Add 3 tablespoons oil, a little at a time and knead into a smooth dough. Divide dough into 20 equal portions. Form each portion into a tiny ball 2½ cms in diameter and keep aside.
Put remaining oil (¾ cup) into cooker. Place brinjals, potatoes and kand in a single layer in cooker. Add beans and bananas. Place prepared balls on top.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight.
Open cooker. Serve hot.
Peel oranges. Lightly scrape off white part inside peel. Finely cut peel into small slivers. Remove pips and tie in a muslin. Cut up fruit, saving juice.
Put fruit along with juice, peel, pips and half the water (2½ cups) into cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Lift out muslin. Squeeze it well against side of cooker to extract juice. Discard cloth. Transfer contents of cooker into a large saucepan.
Add remaining water (2½ cups) and lemon juice. Bring to boil on high heat. Reduce heat to medium. Add sugar gradually, stirring constantly until all sugar is dissolved.
Boil marmalade rapidly on high heat, until setting point is reached.
Remove from heat. Skim off any foam. Allow to cool until skin starts to form. Stir to distribute the peel. Pour marmalade into hot sterile jars. Seal.
Put pineapple, apples and lemon juice into cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Transfer contents into a large saucepan.
Place pan on medium heat. Add sugar gradually, stirring constantly until sugar is completely dissolved. Boil rapidly on high heat until setting point is reached.
Remove pan from heat. Skim off any foam. Pour jam into hot sterile jars. Seal.
Recipe can be reduced to one-half by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Put potatoes, onion, salt and water in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Mix the mixture smooth in a blender or mash it through a sieve.
Return soup to cooker. Add milk and pepper.
Place cooker on medium heat and bring to boil, stirring constantly. Add cheese and stir till cheese melts. Serve immediately, garnished with parsley.
Grind together ginger and garlic into a paste.
Heat oil in a deep frying pan for about 3 minutes. Group arbi into 3 batches. Lightly brown each batch and remove. Strain oil.

Place cooker on high heat with 5 tablespoons oil. Add onions and fry till golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste. Stir for a few seconds. Add tomatoes and all other ingredients except curd, water and garam masala powder. Cook till tomatoes are pulpy, stirring occasionally.

Add 1 tablespoon curd. Stir and fry until curd is well blended (approximately ½ minute). Continue adding remaining curd in the same way, a tablespoon at a time, till all curd is used. Fry till oil shows separately (approximately 3 minutes). Add arbi. Mix well. Add water. Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Stir in garam masala powder. Serve hot.

name: Roasted Gram & Peas Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre

method.instruction: Put gram in the Bowl on medium heat. Stir constantly till gram is copper brown (about 10 minutes). Coarsely grind gram.
Grind onions into a paste.
Grind ginger, garlic and green chilli into a paste.

Heat oil in the Bowl on medium-high heat for about 3 minutes. Add bay leaves, cardamoms, cloves and cinnamon. Stir for a few seconds. Add onion paste and fry till golden brown. Add ginger paste. Stir for a few seconds. Reduce heat to low. Add tomatoes, salt, turmeric, coriander and chilli powders. Cook for about 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add ground gram and peas. Mix. Add water. Stir. Bring to boil on medium-high heat. Reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer till gram and peas are cooked and water is absorbed, stirring occasionally (about 25 minutes).
Add garam masala powder. Stir. Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: When using the 2 Litre Bowl, cook on low or medium heat only.  Cooking on high heat may damage the plastic handles.  For 2 Litre Bowl, reduce the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%).  As this is a smaller vessel than the 3 Litre Bowl, this recipe may cook about 20% faster than the times stated.  Please check after 80% of the stated cooking time to see whether the food is cooked as you would like it and adjust the cooking time as required.
When using the 4 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%) and increase cooking time as required.

name: Sai Bhaji
translation: Spinach with Vegetable and Bengal Gram

name: Samosa
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick 4 Litre Deep-Fry  Pan (Kadhai)

method.instruction: To make dough: Sift together flour and salt into a bowl. Add oil and rub it in with your fingers till mixture resembles fine bread crumbs. Gradually add water, mixing and gathering flour to make a stiff ball. Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 3 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.
To make stuffing: Cut 3 potatoes into ¼ inch/6 mm cubes. Mash 1 potato.

Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes. Add onion. Fry till light brown. Add peas, ginger, green chillies, coriander leaves and water. Stir. Cover and cook on low heat till peas are tender and water has evaporated, stirring occasionally.
Add cut potatoes, salt, coriander, garam masala, cumin, chilli and mango powders. Stir-fry about 2 minutes. Add mashed potatoes and lemon juice. Stir about 1 minute. Allow mixture to cool.
To make samosa: Knead dough about 1 minute. Make 12 balls. On a floured board, roll a ball of dough into a thin flat round 6 inch/15 cm in diameter.

Cut round in half with a sharp knife. Dampen the cut edge of one half with water. Pick up and form a cone, making a ¼ inch/6 mm overlapping seam. Press to seal. Fill cone with 1½ tbsp/22.5 ml stuffing.

Close top of cone by sticking the open edges together with a little water to form a ¼ inch/6 mm seam. Pinch top seam firmly to form a scalloped edge. Cover with damp cloth. Assemble 23 more samosa in the same way. Wash and wipe dry pan thoroughly.

Heat oil in pan on high heat about 8 minutes (160oC). Reduce heat to medium. Add 12 samosa one after the other. Fry till dark golden brown. Remove and drain. Fry remaining samosa in the same way, increasing heat after each batch. Serve hot, accompanied with chutney or tomato ketchup.

special_instructions.instruction: When using 1.5 Litre pan: Use oil for Frying 1¾  cups/420 ml. Heat oil on high heat about 5 minutes, add 4 samosa in each batch nstead of 12.
When using 2.5 Litre pan: Use oil for Frying 3 cups/720 ml. Heat oil on high heat about 6 minutes, add 6 samosa in each batch instead of 12.

name: Samosas
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

name: Sautéed Vegetables Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm

method.instruction: Heat oil in frying pan on medium heat about 2 minutes. Add cumin seeds. Stir a few seconds.

Add onion and ginger. Stir fry till onion is transparent (about 2 minutes). Add peas, chillies and water. Stir. Cover and cook on low heat (medium-low for 30 cm pan) till peas are just tender and water has evaporated (about 10 minutes), stirring occasionally. (If water remains when peas are tender, uncover pan and boil away water.)
Add potatoes and salt. Stir fry about 3 minutes. Add garam masala powder. Mix. Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm Frying Pan: Reduce ingredients by one-third.
For 22 cm (Rounded Sides): No change required.
For 30 cm Frying Pan:Increase ingredients by two-thirds. Step 1: Heat oil about 2½ minutes.

name: Sautéed Vegetables 2 Litre Futura Nonstick Curry Pan
cookware_note: with Glass Lid

name: Sautéed Vegetables Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: Heat oil in frying pan on medium heat no more than 4 minutes (for pan other than 26 cm see

special_instructions.instruction: below).  Add cumin seeds.  Stir a few seconds.

Add onion and ginger.  Stir fry till onion is transparent (about 2 minutes).  Add peas, chillies and water.  Stir.  Cover and cook on low heat till peas are just tender and water has evaporated (about 10 minutes), stirring occasionally.
Add potatoes and salt.  Stir fry about 3 minutes.  Add garam masala powder.  Mix.  Serve hot.
For 18 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Reduce ingredients by one-half.  Step 1:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat no more than 2 minutes.
For 22 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Reduce ingredients by one-third.  Step 1:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat no more than 3 minutes.
For 26 cm pan (rounded sides) ingredients may be increased by one-fourth.
For 30 cm pan, increase ingredients by two-thirds.  Step 1:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat no more than 4 minutes.

method.instruction: To cook reduced quantities : do not reduce water at all.
Pour water into cooker. Put grid in cooker. Place spinach on grid. Sprinkle garlic.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Drain spinach.
In a bowl, combine yogurt, salt and pepper. Add spinach. Mix well. Serve immediately or chilled.

name: Stir - Fried Bean Sprouts and Vegetables Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan
Stir-Fried Bean Sprouts and Vegetables Futura Nonstick 2 Litre Deep-Fry  Pan Stir-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Mix salt, sugar and lemon juice in a bowl. Stir till sugar is dissolved.

Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes. Add ginger and garlic. Stir a few seconds. Increase heat to medium-high. Add carrot. Stir about 1 minute. Add onions. Stir-fry till onions are transparent. Add cabbage, sprouts and green chilli. Stir-fry about 2 minutes, pouring water around the sides of the pan. Add lemon juice mixture. Stir a few seconds. Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: * To sprout whole mung beans: Soak ¼ cup + 1 tbsp/60 g whole mung beans in 1 cup/240 ml water overnight. Drain. Place a wet cloth on a flat plate. Spread mung beans. Fold cloth over mung beans. Cover with another plate. Keep aside 2 days, sprinkling water on cloth to keep it damp (once or twice a day).

name: Stir-Fried Chinese Noodles & Vegetables Futura Nonstick 3 Litre Stir-Fry Wok

method.instruction: To cook reduced quantities : do not reduce water at all.
Cut a hole in the top of each green pepper and lift away the core. Carefully remove and discard seeds and pith.
In a bowl, mix together all ingredients for stuffing (either vegetarian or tuna) except ½ cup cheese.
Divide stuffing into 4 equal portions. Fill each pepper, pressing down well.
Pour water into cooker. Put grid in cooker. Stand peppers on grid.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 1 minute.
Remove cooker from heat. Reduce pressure by placing cooker in approximately 4 inches (10 cm) of cold water in a basin or in a sink for 1 or 2 minutes.
Open cooker. Remove peppers and sprinkle tops with remaining cheese (½ cup).
Place peppers under a broiler till cheese melts. Serve hot.

name: Stuffed Vegetable Cutlets Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: To make stuffing: Melt butter in a pan. Add onion and ginger. Fry till onion is transparent. Add cumin seeds, green chillies, peas, french beans, carrots and ¼ teaspoon salt. Stir for a few seconds. Add ¼ cup water. Stir. Cover and cook on low heat till vegetables are just tender and water has evaporated, stirring occasionally. Add garam masala powder and coriander leaves. Mix. Allow mixture to cool.

Put bread in remaining water (1 cup) for 15 seconds. Squeeze out water. Mix bread, potatoes and remaining salt (2¾ teaspoons). Divide into 36 portions. Make 2 patties 4 cm in diameter using 2 portions. Put 1 teaspoon stuffing in the centre of 1 patty. Cover with second patty. Press edges to seal. Gently pat all around to give an even shape. Assemble remaining patties in the same way.

Heat oil in deep-fry pan on high heat about 5 minutes (190ºC). Add 4 cutlets one after the other. Reduce heat to medium. Fry till golden brown. Remove and drain. Fry remaining cutlets in the same way, increasing heat after each batch. Serve hot, accompanied with chutney or tomato ketchup.

name: Sukhi Bhaji
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi (Saucepan)

method.instruction: Grind together ginger, garlic and green chillies into a paste.

Heat oil in cooker for about 3 minutes. Add onions and fry till golden brown. Add ground paste. Stir for a few seconds. Add tomatoes, salt, turmeric, chilli and garam masala powders. Cook till tomatoes are pulpy and oil shows separately (approximately 5 minutes), stirring occasionally. Add yam and all other ingredients except water. Stir and fry for about 3 minutes. Add water. Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Serve hot.

Heat oil in a pan for about 2 minutes. Add coconut, coriander seeds and curry leaves. Stir fry till coconut is golden brown. Remove and grind into a paste, adding a little water (1 cup) from time to time.
Put 4 cups water, jackfruit seeds, red gram, green chillies, salt, turmeric and chilli powders in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Add drumsticks, tamarind pulp and remaining water (2 cups).

Place cooker on high heat and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and cook till drumsticks are tender (approximately 10 minutes), stirring occasionally. Add ground paste and bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Remove cooker from heat.
For tempering, heat oil in a pan for about 1 minute. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add onions and curry leaves. Fry till onions are transparent and pour over thiyyal. Stir. Serve hot.
Pour water into cooker. Add bananas.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 4 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Remove, peel and grate bananas.

Heat oil in a pan for about 2 minutes. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add Bengal gram and black grams. Stir and fry till golden brown. Add curry leaves, chillies and turmeric powder. Stir for a few seconds. Add bananas and salt. Mix well.
Remove pan from heat. Add coconut. Stir well. Serve hot.
To cook reduced quantities : do not reduce water at all.
Put all ingredients except 1 tbsp parsley in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 2 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Discard bay leaf. Place vegetables on serving dish. Keep hot.
Return cooker to high heat and boil till liquid is reduced to half, stirring occasionally. Pour over vegetables. Serve hot, garnished with remaining parsley (1 tbsp).

name: Vegetable Pakoras
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

name: Vegetable Pilau 3 Litre Futura Nonstick Cook-n-Serve Stewpot with Glass Lid

method.instruction: Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes. Add cumin seeds. Stir a few seconds. Add bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamoms and cloves. Stir a few seconds. Add carrots, beans, peas and potatoes. Stir fry a few seconds. Add rice. Stir fry about 2 minutes. Add salt and water. Stir. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer till rice is cooked and water is absorbed (about 20 minutes). Lift rice gently with a fork to mix pilau. Serve  ot.

special_instructions.instruction: When using the 5 litre pan, the quantity of ingredients may be increased by two-thirds.

name: Vegetable Rolls on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava

method.instruction: Put all ingredients in cooker.  Stir.
Close cooker.  Bring to full pressure on high heat.  Reduce heat and cook 15 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat.  Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker.  Strain stock through a fine sieve.

name: Vegetarian Snacks on Toothpicks Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Melt butter in cooker. Add onion and leeks. Cook on low heat until soft but not brown, stirring occasionally. Add potatoes, salt and water.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Add milk and half and half. Stir.
Place cooker on medium heat and bring to boil, stirring frequently.

Mix the soup smooth in a blender or mash it through a sieve. Allow to cool to room temperature, stirring occasionally. Strain soup through a fine sieve. Stir in heavy cream and refrigerate until well chilled (approximately 6 hours). Serve cold, garnished with chives or green onion.

special_instructions.instruction: To cook reduced quantities: reduce all ingredients proportionately but not below one-fourth.

method.instruction: Recipe can be reduced to one-half by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Put all ingrdients except stock in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 1 minute.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. In a blender, Pure'e mixture very lightly and briefly ensuring that small pieces of zucchini remain.
Return soup to cooker. Add stock. Stir.
Place cooker on medium heat and bring to boil, stirring occasionally. Serve hot. Alternatively, refrigerate until well chilled (approximately 4 hours) and serve cold.

name: Alu Paratha
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Flat Tava 30 cm
cooking_time: , mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky).  Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes).  Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.

method.instruction: To make filling:  Mix all Filling ingredients.
Rub hands with a little ghee.  Make 12 balls about 1¾ inch/4.5 cm in diameter.  Keep covered.

To make dough:  Mix flour, salt, ghee and 1 cup/240 ml  water.  Add enough of remaining water (½ cup/120 ml), 2 tbsp/30 ml at a time, mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky).  Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes).  Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.
Rub hands with a little ghee.  Knead dough briefly.  Make 12 balls about 1½ inch/3.8 cm in diameter.  Keep covered with a damp cloth.

On a floured board, roll each ball of dough into a flat round 4¾ inch/12 cm in diameter. (To prevent sticking when rolling out rounds of dough:  slightly flatten balls and press the balls/coils lightly in flour on both sides.  Shake off excess flour and roll out.)  Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.

To stuff paratha:  Place round on palm of hand.  Cup hand slightly to form a depression in centre of round (Figure A).  Put a ball of filling on round (Figure B).  Surround filling evenly with dough by gently opening and closing hand slightly in a pulsating motion till some edges just meet.  Pinch together the edges which meet just enough to seal (Figure C).  Pat sealed area to get an even thickness of dough.  Repeat till round is closed fully.
Place stuffed round on board.  Flatten slightly.  Stuff and place remaining rounds of dough in the same way as given in step 6.  Keep covered with a damp cloth.
On a floured board, roll each stuffed ball gently into a flat round 7¼ inch/18 cm in diameter.  Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.
Heat tava on medium heat no more than 5 minutes (for preheating tava other than 30 cm, see

special_instructions.instruction: Put round on tava.  Cook as explained below.  Cook remaining rounds in the same way.  Serve hot, accompanied with curd. a Put round on preheated tava.  Cook 1 minute.  Top should begin to look dry and darken.  (There should be a few light brown specks on the underside.) b Turn over with a wooden spatula.  (If you can adjust the heat so that a few light brown specks appear on the underside in 1 minute, you will be cooking at the ideal temperature.) c Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml ghee all over top surface of round. d Turn over.  Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml ghee in the same way. e Turn over.  Lightly press entire round with spatula, rotating and pressing a small area at a time.  (Pressing paratha ensures even cooking.  If you notice a lighter area, press on the opposite side of that area when you have turned over paratha.) f Turn over.  Press in the same way and spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml ghee over round. g Turn over.  Continue pressing and turning till paratha is evenly browned (medium-brown) on both sides.  (Paratha and stuffed paratha take about 5 minutes to cook.) h For instructions on how to stuff paratha: see Alu Paratha.
For 22 cm tava, in Step 9, heat tava on medium heat no more than 3 minutes.
For 26 cm tava, in Step 9, heat tava on medium heat no more than 4 minutes.

name: Appam
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Appachaty

method.instruction: To make coconut milk: Put 2 cups/160 g coconut and hot water in a mixer-blender/grinder and mix the mixture about 3 minutes. Place muslin cloth over a bowl. Put mixture into muslin cloth; allow to cool till it is comfortable to handle. Gather up the corners and squeeze through as much milk as possible. Measure 1½ cups/360 ml milk and keep aside.
Grind into a paste rice, cooked rice and remaining coconut (2 cups/160 g), gradually adding coconut milk.
In a large bowl, mix rice paste, sugar and salt. Cover and keep aside (not in a refrigerator) at least 12 hours to ferment.

Heat pan on medium heat no more than 2 minutes. Stir batter and pour 1/3 cup/80 ml in centre of pan. With both hands lift pan from heat. Tilt and rotate pan to spread batter to make a round about 6½ inch/16 cm in diameter. Return pan to heat. (The batter which slides/flows back to the centre produces the desired fluffy, soft centre which is thicker than the "lacy" edges.) Cover and cook till surface appears dry (about 1½ minutes). Remove. Cook remaining appam in the same way. Serve hot with vegetable/chicken stew or Coconut Chutney.
Recipe can be reduced to one-third by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Pour water into cooker. Bring to boil on high heat. Add salt and rice. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally for 5 minutes. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Fluff up rice gently with a fork to separate grains.

name: Besan Poora
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm

name: Besan Poora
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: Sift together besan and salt into a bowl.  Gradually add water, stirring constantly to make a smooth batter.  Add all other ingredients except oil.  Mix.
Heat frying pan on medium heat about 3 minutes (for pan other than 26 cm see

special_instructions.instruction: below).  Add ¼ tsp/1.3 ml oil and spread over inside base with wooden spatula.  Stir and pour ¼ cup/60 ml batter in centre of pan.  Place the back of a rounded ladle very lightly on batter.  Slowly and gently spread batter evenly outwards from centre to make a round about 5½ inch/14 cm in diameter.  Add 1 tsp/5 ml oil around edges of poora.  Dribble ¼ tsp/1.3 ml oil over centre.  Fry till underside is golden brown and slightly crisp (about 1½ minutes).  Turn over.  Press all over top surface of poora with spatula.*  Fry till golden brown and slightly crisp (about 1½ minutes).  Remove.  Fry remaining poora in the same way. Serve hot. * Pressing makes poora crisper, as well as ensuring even cooking and browning. For a softer poora omit pressing.
For 18 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Step 2: Heat pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.  Stir and pour 3 tbsp/45 ml batter in centre of pan.  Make a round about 4 inch/10 cm in diameter.
For 22 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Step 2: Heat pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 30 cm pan, in Step 2, heat pan on medium heat about 3 minutes.  Stir and pour 1/3 cup/80 ml batter to make 6¾ inch/17 cm poora.

method.instruction: Pour water into cooker. Bring to boil on high heat. Add rice. Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Serve hot.

name: Chana Dal Paratha
translation: Unleavened Bread Stuffed with Bengal Gram
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Tava (Griddle)
cooking_time: , mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.

method.instruction: To make filling: Bring water to boil in a medium pan on high heat. Add chana dal, salt and turmeric. Stir. Bring to boil. Reduce heat to low. Cover and cook till dal is tender but not mushy (about 35 minutes). Meanwhile, do step 2. Drain off excess water, if any. Allow to cool. Add remaining ingredients. Mix.

To make dough: Mix flour, salt, ghee and 1 cup/240 ml water. Add enough of remaining water (¼ cup + 2 tbsp/90 ml), 2 tbsp/30 ml at a time, mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.
Rub hands with a little ghee. Knead dough briefly. Make 12 balls about 1½ inch/3.8 cm in diameter. Keep covered with a damp cloth.

On a floured board, roll each ball of dough into a flat round 4 inch/10 cm in diameter (To prevent sticking when rolling out rounds of dough: slightly flatten balls and press the balls lightly in flour on both sides. Shake off excess flour and roll out). Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.
To stuff paratha: Follow illustrated steps using ¼ cup/60 ml filling for each round of dough.
Place round on palm of hand. Cup hand slightly to form a depression in centre of round (Figure A).
Put ¼ cup/60 ml filling on round. Lightly pat filling, spreading evenly to ¼ inch/6 mm from edge of round (Figure B).

Surround filling evenly with dough by gently opening and closing hand slightly in a pulsating motion till some edges just meet. Pinch together the edges which meet just enough to seal (Figure C). Pat sealed area to get an even thickness of dough. Repeat till round is closed fully.
Place stuffed round on board. Flatten slightly. Stuff remaining rounds of dough in the same way. Keep covered with a damp cloth.
On a floured board, roll each stuffed ball gently into a flat round 6 inch/15 cm in diameter. Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.
Heat tava on medium heat about 1½ minutes for 22 cm tava/about 3 minutes for 26 cm tava.
Put round on tava. Cook as explained below. Cook remaining rounds in the same way. Serve hot, accompanied with curd.

Put round on preheated tava. Cook 1 minute. (Top should begin to look dry and darken. There should be a few light brown specks on the underside. If you can adjust the heat so that a few brown specks appear on the underside in 1 minute, you will be cooking at the ideal temperature.) Turn over round with a broad spatula.
Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml ghee all over top surface of round. Turn over.
Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml ghee in the same way. Turn over.

Lightly press entire round with spatula, rotating and pressing a small area at a time. (Pressing paratha ensures even cooking. If you notice a lighter area, press on the opposite side of that area when you have turned over paratha) Turn over.
Press in the same way. Turn over.
Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml ghee over round. Turn over.
Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml ghee over round. Turn over.
Continue pressing and turning till paratha is evenly browned (medium-brown) on both sides. Remove.
Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add bay leaves, peppercorns, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon and cumin seeds. Stir for a few seconds.
Add gram. Stir fry for about 3 minutes.
Add rice. Stir fry till rice turns opaque(approximately 3 minutes). Add salt and water. Stir
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally for 5 minutes. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open Cooker. Fluff up rice gently with a fork to separate grains. Serve hot.

name: Channa Dal Paratha
translation: Unleavened Bread Stuffed with Bengal Gram
cookware_note: on Futura Nonstick Tava (Griddle) 26 cm

method.instruction: Recipe can be reduced to one-third by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Group chicken into 2 batches. Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Brown and remove each batch onto paper towels to drain off excess oil.
Away from heat, sprinkle water in cooker. Stir to remove any frying residues attached to base.
Return cooker to heat. Add onion, garlic and sausages. Stir fry till onion is transparent. Add rice and fry till rice turns opaque (approximately 3 minutes).
Remove cooker from heat. Add chicken and remaining ingredients. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 6 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally for 5 minutes. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Serve hot.
Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add bay leaves, peppercorns, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon and cumin seeds. Stir for a few seconds.
Add Bengal gram. Stir and fry for about 3 minutes.
Add rice. Fry till rice turns opaque (approximately 3 minutes), stirring constantly. Add salt and water. Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally for 5 minutes. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight.
Open cooker. Serve hot.

method.instruction: In a bowl, cover rice and urad dal with water at least 1 inch/2.5 cm above ingredients. Soak 4 hours. Drain.
Grind rice-dal mixture into a paste gradually adding 1¼ cups/300 ml water.
In a large bowl, mix rice-dal paste and salt. Cover and keep aside (not in a refrigerator) at least 12 hours to ferment.
Mix fermented paste and enough of remaining water (¼ cup + 2½ tbsp/100 ml) to make a thin batter.
Heat tava on medium heat no more than 5 minutes.

Stir batter and pour ½ cup/120 ml in centre of tava. Quickly place the flat base of a metal ladle or katori very lightly on batter. Cook the first dosa about 3 minutes. During this time, add ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil around edges of dosa and dribble ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil all over dosa. Turn over. Cook remaining dosa in the same way as the first dosa except reduce cooking time before turning over dosa from about 3 minutes to about 2 minutes. Serve hot, accompanied with Coconut Chutney.

Mix fermented paste and enough of remaining water (½ cup/120 ml) to make a thin batter.
Heat tava on medium heat no more than 5 minutes (for preheating tava other than 30 cm, see special instructions).

name: French Toast Futura Nonstick Flat Tava 30 cm

method.instruction: In a bowl, beat eggs just till yolks and whites are mixed.  Add milk, sugar, vanilla essence and salt.  Mix.
Turn 1 slice bread in egg mixture leaving each side in mixture about 20 seconds.  Remove with a spatula and put on a plate.  Turn and remove 3 more slices in the same way.
Heat tava on medium heat about 4 minutes (for preheating tava other than 30 cm, see special instructions).  Spread 2 tsp/10 ml butter on tava all over the area where the slices will be placed.

Add the 4 soaked slices.  Pour egg mixture leftover on the plate back into the bowl.  Fry on both sides till browned (2-3 minutes on each side).  Remove.  (While slices are frying turn 4 more slices in egg mixture as described in step 2 and keep aside.)

Spread 2 tsp/10 ml butter on tava.  Fry the soaked slices in the same way as in step 4.  Adjust heat to medium-low* if tava is too hot for the second batch.  Serve hot with honey or jam. (* A heat setting between medium and low.)

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm tava, in Step 2, each batch is 1 slice.  Step 3:  Heat tava on medium heat about 2 minutes and reduce heat to low.  Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml butter on tava where slice will be placed.

For 26 cm tava, in Step 2,  first two batches are 3 slices each.  Step 3:  Heat tava on medium heat about 3 minutes.  Spread 1½ tsp/7.5 ml butter on tava where slices will be placed.  Step 5:  Remaining batch:  1 tsp/5 ml butter; 2 slices.

name: Khichuri
translation: Soft Rice with Green Gram and Vegetables

method.instruction: Grind ginger and garlic into a paste.
Grind cardamoms, cinnamon and cloves into a paste adding a little water (1 tsp) from time to time.
In a pan, roast green gram on medium heat till golden brown (approximately 7 minutes), stirring constantly. Remove and wash gram.

Heat oil in cooker for about 5 minutes. Add bay leaves and cumin seeds. Stir for a few seconds. Add onion and fry till golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste. Stir for a few seconds. Add gram and all other ingredients except water, spice paste and ghee. Mix. Add remaining water (8 cups). Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 4 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Add spice paste. Stir. Pour ghee evenly over khichuri. Serve hot.
Pour water into cooker. Bring to boil on high heat. Add oil and 1½ teaspoons salt. Add macaroni gradually, stirring. Close cooker.

Place cooker on medium heat. Start making cheese sauce as explained in step 3. Meanwhile, when the cooker comes to full pressure reduce heat and cook for 4 minutes. Remove cooker from heat. Reduce pressure by placing cooker in a basin of cold water for about 2 minutes.

In a pan, melt butter on medium heat. Stir in flour and cook without discolouring (approximately 1 minute). Add milk gradually, stirring constantly with a wire whisk. Boil till sauce thickens (approximately 2 minutes). Remove pan from heat. Stir in cheese, pepper and remaining salt (1 teaspoon). Keep hot.
Open cooker. Drain macaroni. Serve hot, topped with cheese sauce.

method.instruction: Bring 8 cups water to boil in Handi on high heat. Add rice and salt. Stir. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to medium. Boil till rice is half cooked (about 10 minutes). Drain rice in a colander. Wash and dry Handi.
In a large saucepan, put remaining water (½ cup), milk and saffron. Bring to boil on high heat. Add sugar. Stir constantly till sugar is dissolved. Add rice. Mix. Remove from heat.
Heat ghee in Handi on medium heat for about 3 minutes. Add cardamom and rice mixture. Stir. Bring to boil (about 3 minutes).
Reduce heat to very low. Cover. Simmer for 20 minutes, or till rice is cooked and liquid is absorbed.
Place meethe chawal in serving bowl. Sprinkle almonds. Serve hot.

Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamoms and cloves. Stir for a few seconds. Add carrots, beans and peas. Stir fry for about 2 minutes. Add rice. Stir fry till rice turns opaque (approximately 3 minutes). Add salt and water. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally for 5 minutes. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Serve hot.

method.instruction: Sift together flour, baking powder and salt, three times. Place in a bowl.
In a separate bowl, mix egg, milk and butter.
Pour milk mixture into flour mixture. Stir quickly till all the flour is just moistened. Do not break the lumps.

Heat frying pan on medium-high heat about 1½ minutes to the correct temperature. To test pan for correct temperature: sprinkle a few drops water in pan. If water just sits and boils, pan is not hot enough. If water forms into tiny balls that sit like beads of mercury, pan is too hot. If water forms tiny balls which bounce and skitter before vaporising, pan is ready to use. Reduce heat to medium.

Pour ¼ cup/60 ml mixture into pan and pour again another ¼ cup/60 ml mixture into pan to make 2 separate cakes. Bubbles will appear on the upper sides and pop. Cook till edges appear dry (about 2 minutes). Bottom surface should be golden brown. Turn pancakes (only once). Cook second side till golden brown (about 2 minutes). Remove. Serve hot immediately, with butter and honey or maple syrup.
Cook and serve remaining pancakes in the same way as in step 5.
