On a floured board, roll each ball of dough into a flat round 7¼ inch/18 cm in diameter.  Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.

Heat tava on medium heat no more than 5 minutes (for preheating tava other than 30 cm,  see special instructions).  Put round on tava.  Cook till small blisters appear on surface and very few light brown specks appear on underside (about 50 seconds).  Turn over.  Cook till brown specks appear on underside (about 30 seconds).  Turn over.  If you are experienced with the technique, you may puff up phulka directly on the flame.  Otherwise, with a folded kitchen cloth, press edges of phulka till phulka puffs up and/or underside has brown specks (about 1 minute).    Cook remaining rounds in the same way.  Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm tava, in Step 4, heat tava on medium heat no more than 3 minutes.
For 26 cm tava, in Step 4, heat tava on medium heat no more than 4 minutes.

name: Phulka
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Tava (Griddle)

name: Phulka
cookware_note: on Futura Nonstick Tava (Griddle) 26 cm
cooking_time: , mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.

method.instruction: Mix flour, salt and ½ cup water. Add enough of remaining water (6 tablespoons), 2 tablespoons at a time, mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.
With oiled hands, knead dough briefly. Make 12 balls about 3 cm in diameter. Keep covered with a damp cloth.

On a floured board, roll each ball of dough into a flat round 14 cm in diameter (To prevent  sticking when rolling out rounds of dough: slightly flatten balls and press the balls lightly in flour on both sides. Shake off excess flour and roll out). Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.

Heat tava on medium heat about 2 minutes for 22 cm tava/about 3 minutes for 26 cm tava. Put round on tava. Cook till small blisters appear on surface and very few light brown specks appear on underside (about 15-20 seconds). Turn over. Cook till brown specks appear on underside (about 50 seconds). Turn over. If you are experienced with the technique, you may puff up phulka directly on the flame. Otherwise, with a folded kitchen cloth, press edges of phulka till phulka puffs up and/or underside has brown specks (about 30-40 seconds). Cook remaining rounds in the same way. Serve hot.

name: Pudina Paratha
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Tava (Griddle)

name: Pudina Paratha
cookware_note: on Futura Nonstick Tava (Griddle) 26 cm
cooking_time: , mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.

method.instruction: Mix all ingredients except water and ghee. Add ¾ cup water. Mix. Add enough of remaining water (½ cup), 2 tablespoons at a time, mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 2 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.
2. Rub hands with a little ghee. Knead dough briefly. Make 12 balls about 4 cm in diameter. Keep covered with a damp cloth.

On a floured board, roll a ball of dough into a flat round 15 cm in diameter (To prevent  sticking when rolling out rounds of dough: slightly flatten balls and press the balls/coils lightly in flour on both sides. Shake off excess flour and roll out). Spread ½  teaspoon ghee all over the top surface of the round.

Starting at one edge, tightly roll up round (Figure A). With palms of both hands roll back and forth till extended to 20 cm long (Figure B). Wind around one end into a flat coil (Figure C). Put free end over centre top of coil (Figure D). Press down free end gently. Flatten coil slightly.
Make remaining balls into coils in the same way. Keep covered with a damp cloth.
On a floured board, roll each coil into a flat round 15 cm in diameter. Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.
Heat tava on medium heat about 2 minutes for 22 cm tava/about 3 minutes for 26 cm tava.
Put round on tava. Cook as explained below.
Put round on preheated tava. Cook 1½ minutes. Top should begin to look dry and darken. (There should be a few light brown specks on the underside.)
Turn over with a wooden spatula. (If you can adjust the heat so that a few light brown specks appear on the underside in 1½ minutes, you will be cooking at the ideal temperature.)
Spread ½ teaspoon ghee all over top surface of round.
Turn over. Spread ½ teaspoon ghee in the same way.

Turn over. Lightly press entire round with spatula, rotating and pressing a small area at a time. (Pressing paratha ensures even cooking. If you notice a lighter area, press on the opposite side of that area when you have turned over paratha.)
Turn over. Press in the same way.
Turn over. Spread ½ teaspoon ghee over round.
Turn over. Spread ½ teaspoon ghee over round.
Turn over. Continue pressing and turning till paratha is evenly browned (medium-brown) on both sides. (Paratha take about 4 minutes to cook.)

Remove paratha from tava. With paper napkins or cloth protecting both hands, cup hands around paratha and quickly bring together (crushing paratha) and release. Rotate paratha 90 degrees. Repeat crushing. Cook remaining rounds in the same way. Serve hot, accompanied with curd.

name: Puran Poli
cookware_note: on Futura Nonstick Tava (Griddle) 26 cm

name: Puran Poli
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Tava (Griddle)
cooking_time: , kneading after each addition, till dough is very soft, elastic and no longer sticky. Keep covered 1 hour.

method.instruction: To make dough: Mix flour, salt and turmeric. Add water and mix to make a stiff dough. Add oil, 2 tbsp/30 ml at a time, kneading after each addition, till dough is very soft, elastic and no longer sticky. Keep covered 1 hour.

To make filling: Put chana dal and water in a Hawkins pressure cooker. Close cooker. Bring to full pressure (first whistle) on high heat. Reduce heat and cook 10 minutes. Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally
Open cooker. Mash dal with back of a ladle.
Add gur and cardamom. Cook on medium heat till mixture thickens and leaves the sides of cooker, stirring occasionally (about 15 minutes). Allow to cool.
With oiled hands, make 18 balls of filling about 1¼ inch/3 cm in diameter. Keep covered.
To make poli: Knead dough briefly. With oiled hands, make 18 balls of dough about 1¼ inch/ 3 cm in diameter. Keep covered.

Rub hands with a little oil. Put a ball of dough on palm of one hand. With fingers of other hand, pat dough outwards to form a round 2½ inch/6 cm in diameter. Put a ball of filling in centre of round. Close round over filling by gently gathering dough from sides to top, pinching to form a tiny peak. Press peak to one side and pat dough to seal any openings. Keep assembled ball on floured surface. Assemble and keep remaining balls in the same way.

On a floured board, flatten assembled ball slightly. Roll gently into a flat round 5½ inch/14 cm in diameter (To prevent sticking when rolling out rounds of dough: slightly flatten balls and press the balls/coils lightly in flour on both sides. Shake off excess flour and roll out.). Roll out remaining balls in the same way. Keep covered.

Heat tava on medium heat about 1½ minutes for 22 cm tava/about 3 minutes for 26 cm tava. Put round on tava. Cook 1 minute (underside should have very few faint brown specks). Turn over. Cook second side similarly 1 minute. Remove. Cook remaining rounds in the same way. Serve hot or at room temperature. Optional garnish: melted ghee or butter.

name: Puris
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add garlic and fry till golden brown. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add cardamoms, peppercorns, cinnamon, cloves, curry leaves, green chillies and caraway seeds. Stir for a few seconds.

Add onions and fry for about 3 minutes. Add carrots, beans and peas. Stir and fry for about 2 minutes. Add tomatoes. Stir and fry for about 1 minute. Add rice and salt. Mix well. Add water. Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally 5 minutes. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight.
Open cooker. Serve hot.

name: Rawa Dosa
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Flat Tava 30 cm

name: Rawa Dosa
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Flat Tava (Griddle)

method.instruction: Grind urad dal into a paste gradually adding 1¼  cups/300 ml water.

Put rawa in a large bowl. Gradually add remaining water (1¾ cups + 2 tbsp/450 ml), stirring constantly to make a smooth batter. Add dal paste and salt. Mix. Cover and keep aside (not in a refrigerator) at least 12 hours to ferment.
Rub oil all over the inside base of tava using a clean cotton cloth or paper napkin. Heat tava on medium-high heat till oil just begins to smoke (about 4 minutes). Reduce heat to medium.

Stir batter and pour 3 tbsp/45 ml in centre of tava. Quickly place the back of a rounded ladle very lightly on batter. Using a continuous circular motion in one direction, gently spread batter evenly outwards from centre to make a round about 7½ inch/19 cm in diameter. Add ½ tsp/2.5 ml butter mainly on dosa all around its edge, letting some butter flow onto tava. Dribble ½ tsp/2.5 ml butter all over dosa. Cook about 2 minutes. Turn over. Cook 1 minute. Remove. Cook remaining dosa in the same way except do not add oil on tava before pouring batter. Serve hot, accompanied with Coconut Chutney.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm Flat Tava (Griddle):  Step 4: Make a round about 7¼ inch/18 cm in diameter. Dribble ¼ tsp/1.3 ml butter all over dosa. Follow all other instructions in this step.

name: Rawa Dosa
cookware_note: on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava

name: Sabudana Wadas
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Wash and drain sago. Keep aside for 1½ hours. Stir thoroughly. Keep aside another 1½ hours.

Place groundnuts in Futura or other heavy frying pan on medium heat. Stir constantly till groundnuts darken by a few shades to brown and give off an aroma (about 5 minutes). Remove from pan. Allow to cool. Remove skins by rubbing groundnuts. Break groundnuts into 3 mm - 4 mm pieces.
Grind ginger and cumin seeds into a paste.
Mix ginger-cumin paste and all other ingredients. Knead mixture just enough to bind. Make 20 patties 6 cm in diameter, about ¾ cm thick.

Heat oil in deep-fry pan on high heat for about 5 minutes (190ºC). Add 6 patties one after the other. Reduce heat to medium-low. Fry till light golden brown. Remove and drain. Fry remaining patties in the same way, increasing heat after each batch. Serve hot, accompanied with chutney.

name: Sevian Kheer
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi (Saucepan)

name: Sooji Halwa
cookware_note: 3 Litre Futura Nonstick Cook-n-Serve Bowl with Glass Lid

method.instruction: Heat ghee in The Bowl on medium heat about 3 minutes. Add sooji. Stir fry till light brown (about 15 minutes).
Reduce heat to medium-low. Add water, raisins and half each of almonds and cardamom. Cook till water is absorbed, stirring constantly.
Add sugar. Cook, stirring constantly, till ghee just begins to show on sides (about 10 minutes).
Sprinkle remaining almonds and cardamom. Cover and serve hot.

name: Sooji Halwa
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre

method.instruction: Pour water in cooker.  Bring to boil on high heat.  Add rice and remaining ingredients except cheese.  Mix.
Close cooker.  Bring to full pressure on high heat.  Reduce heat and cook 6 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat.  Allow to cool naturally 5 minutes. Press fingure tip control / lift pressure regulator lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker.  Add cheese.  Stir.  Serve hot.

name: Spanish Rice Casserole Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre
Sukhi Dal
cookware_note: 3 Litre Futura Nonstick Saucepan with Glass Lid

method.instruction: In a bowl, cover dal with water at least 1 inch/2.5 cm above dal. Soak 1 hour. Drain.

Heat 1½ tbsp/22.5 ml ghee in pan on medium heat about 3 minutes. Add chopped onion and stir fry till transparent (about 1 minute). Add dal, salt, turmeric, red chilli and coriander powders. Stir fry about 3 minutes.
Add tomato and green chillies. Mix. Add water. Stir. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to very low*. Cover and simmer 10 minutes

*The low heat setting of the small burner of a gas stove should give very low heat. If your stove is not capable of giving very low heat, use a tava between the flame and the pan or use a suitable metallic ring on the pan supports to increase the distance between the flame and the pan.
Scatter sliced onion over dal.
In a small pan, heat remaining ghee (1½ tbsp/22.5 ml) on medium heat about 1 minute. Pour ghee over dal in pan. Sprinkle coriander leaves.
Cover and cook about 10 minutes, or till dal is cooked. Serve hot with paratha.

name: Three-at-One-Time Masala Uttapam
cookware_note: on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava

name: Traditional South Indian Dosa
cookware_note: on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava
cooking_time: , add ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil around edges of dosa and dribble ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil all over dosa. Turn over. Cook 1 minute, pressing gently with spatula all over dosa to flatten. Remove. Keep heat at medium-high. Cook remaining dosa in the same way as the first dosa except reduce cooking time before turning over dosa from about 3 minutes to about 2 minutes. Serve hot, accompanied with Coconut Chutney.

method.instruction: In a bowl, cover rice, parboiled rice and urad dal with water at least 1 inch/2.5 cm above ingredients. Soak 4 hours. Drain.
Grind rice-dal mixture into a paste gradually adding 2 cups/480 ml water.
In a large bowl, mix rice-dal paste and salt. Cover and keep aside (not in a refrigerator) at least 12 hours to ferment.
Mix fermented paste and enough of remaining water (½ cup/120 ml) to make a thin batter.
Heat tava on medium heat no more than 5 minutes.

Stir batter and pour ½ cup/120 ml in centre of tava. Quickly place the flat base of a metal ladle or katori very lightly on batter. Using a continuous circular motion in one direction, gently spread batter evenly outwards from centre to make a round about 12 inch/30 cm in diameter (see Figure F). Increase heat to medium-high. Cook the first dosa about 3 minutes. During this time, add ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil around edges of dosa and dribble ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil all over dosa. Turn over. Cook 1 minute, pressing gently with spatula all over dosa to flatten. Remove. Keep heat at medium-high. Cook remaining dosa in the same way as the first dosa except reduce cooking time before turning over dosa from about 3 minutes to about 2 minutes. Serve hot, accompanied with Coconut Chutney.

name: Uttapam
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: In separate bowls, cover rice and urad dal with water at least 1 inch/2.5 cm above ingredients.  Soak 4 hours.  Drain.
Grind rice into a paste, gradually adding 1/3 cup/80 ml water.
Grind dal into a paste, gradually adding remaining water (1/3 cup + 2 tsp/90 ml).
In a large bowl, mix rice and dal pastes and salt to make batter.  Cover and keep aside (not in a refrigerator) at least 12 hours to ferment.
Mix fermented paste and all other ingredients except oil.
Heat frying pan on medium heat about 3 minutes (for pan other than 26 cm see

special_instructions.instruction: below).  Stir and pour ½ cup/120 ml batter in centre of pan.  With the back of a rounded ladle, quickly and gently spread batter to make a round of even thickness about 5½ inch/14 cm in diameter.  Add ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil around edges of uttapam.  Fry till surface appears dull and underside is golden brown (about 3 minutes).  Dribble 1 tsp/5 ml oil over uttapam.  Turn over.  Fry till underside is golden brown (about 3 minutes).  Remove.  Fry remaining uttapam in the same way.  Serve hot, accompanied with Coconut Chutney.

For 18 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Step 6:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.  Before pouring batter, reduce heat to medium-low.  Stir and pour 1/3 cup/80 ml batter in centre of pan.  Make a round about 4¾ inch/12 cm in diameter.
For 22 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Step 6:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 30 cm pan, in Step 6, heat oil in pan on medium heat about 3 minutes.  Stir and pour ¾ cup/180 ml batter in centre of pan.  Make a round about 7¼ inch/18 cm in diameter.

name: Uttapam
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Flat Tava 30 cm

name: Uttapam
cookware_note: on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava

method.instruction: In separate bowls, cover rice and urad dal with water at least 1 inch/2.5 cm above ingredients. Soak 4 hours. Drain.
Grind rice into a paste, gradually adding ½ cup/120 ml water.
Grind dal into a paste, gradually adding remaining water (½ cup + 2 tbsp/150 ml).
In a large bowl, mix rice and dal pastes and salt to make batter. Cover and keep aside (not in a refrigerator) at least 12 hours to ferment.
Mix fermented batter gently.
Heat tava on medium heat no more than 6 minutes.

Stir batter and pour ¾ cup/180 ml in centre of tava. With the flat base of a metal ladle, quickly and gently spread batter to make a round of even thickness about 8 inch/20 cm in diameter. Add ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil around edges of uttapam. Fry till surface becomes dull and underside is golden brown (about 3 minutes). Dribble 1 tsp/5 ml oil all over uttapam. Turn over. Fry till underside is golden brown (about 3 minutes). Remove. Fry remaining uttapam in the same way. Serve hot, accompanied with Coconut Chutney.

name: Uttapam
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Flat Tava (Griddle)

method.instruction: Grind cloves, cardamoms, cinnamon and peppercorns into a coarse powder.

Heat ghee in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add onions and fry till light brown. Add jaggery, 2 tbsp water and stir till mixture is brown. Add powdered spices. Stir for a few seconds. Add rice and salt. Stir fry for about 3 minutes. Add remaining water (4 ½ cups). Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally for 5 minutes. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Serve hot.
Grind into a paste ginger, garlic and chillies, gradually adding 2 tbsp/30 ml water.
In a small bowl, stir saffron and milk. Keep aside.

Pour 5 cups/1.2 litres water in cooker. Bring to boil on high heat with lid closed without the vent weight. Open cooker. Add salt (2½ tsp/12.5 ml), cardamoms, cloves, bay leaves, cinnamon and rice. Stir.

Bring to boil in open cooker body. Reduce heat to medium. Place lid on top of the mouth of cooker body such that there is a gap of about 3/8 inch/1 cm from the edge of the lid to the cooker body near the subsidiary handle (see sketch below). Cook till rice just turns opaque (about 5 minutes).
Remove lid. Remove cooker from heat. Immediately transfer rice to a colander and drain. Wash and wipe dry cooker.

Heat ¼ cup/60 ml ghee in cooker about 2 minutes. Add onions and fry till golden brown. Add ginger paste. Stir a few seconds. Add potatoes and cauliflower. Stir and fry about 3 minutes. Add beans, carrots and peas. Stir and fry about 2 minutes. Add red chilli, cumin and garam masala powders. Mix. Add tomatoes, coriander and mint leaves, lemon juice and remaining salt (2½ tsp/12.5 ml). Fry about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add paneer. Mix gently. Add remaining water (1 cup/240 ml). Stir.
Remove cooker from heat. Place rice evenly on vegetables. Dribble saffron milk over rice.
In a small pan, heat remaining ghee (1 tbsp/15 ml) on medium heat about 2 minutes. Add cumin seeds. When cumin seeds begin to darken, immediately pour over rice in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat on gas/on medium-high heat on induction cooktop. Reduce heat and cook 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally 5 minutes. Release pressure with slight lifting of vent weight.
Open cooker. Place rice on a serving dish. Put vegetables and gravy on rice. Garnish with almonds and mint leaves.
Serve hot with butter or curds.

name: Vegetable Pilau Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi

method.instruction: Heat ghee in Handi on medium heat for about 2 minutes. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves. cinnamon, cardamoms and cloves. Stir for a few seconds. Add rice. Stir fry till rice is opaque (about 3 minutes). Add remaining ingredients. Mix. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low. Cover. Simmer for 20 minutes, or till rice is cooked and water is absorbed. Serve hot with curd.

name: Ven Pongal
translation: Rice Cooked with Split Green Gram

name: Welsh Griddle Cakes on Futura Nonstick Tava
cookware_note: 26 cm

method.instruction: Sift together flour, baking powder, salt and nutmeg, three times. Place in large bowl.

Add butter into bowl. Take a small portion of butter and flour mixture between fingertips and thumb of each hand. Raise hands about 20 cm from base of bowl. Rub butter and flour mixture between fingers and thumbs; let mixture fall into bowl. Repeat till all butter is rubbed in and mixture resembles fine bread crumbs. Add sugar and raisins. Mix with a fork. Add egg. Mix with fork. Add and mix enough milk, ½ teaspoon at a time, till mixture can be patted to form a ball. Do not knead.

On a floured surface, roll out dough evenly to about ½ cm thick. Cut into rounds with pastry cutter or inverted bowl or glass 7 cm in diameter. Use a blunt knife/spatula to lift rounds and place on a floured surface.
Gather trimmings and form a ball. Repeat step 3 till all dough is cut into rounds (about 15).
Heat tava on medium heat about 2 minutes for 22 cm tava/about 3 minutes for 26 cm tava.

Reduce heat to low. Spread butter all over tava. Quickly add 5 rounds*(for 22 cm tava click here), leaving the centre space free. Cook about 5 minutes on each side (both sides should be golden brown). Cook remaining rounds, 5* at a time, on low heat about 3 minutes on each side without adding butter. Serve hot or at room temperature accompanied with honey or jam.

special_instructions.instruction: 4 rounds on the 22 cm tava.

name: Welsh Griddle Cakes on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Tava

method.instruction: Put berries and 5 cups water in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 15 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Empty berries into a colander.

Melt butter in a large skillet. Add garlic and stir for a few seconds. Add zucchini and stir fry for about 3 minutes. Add red pepper. Cook, stirring frequently, till zucchini is crisp-tender (approximately 7 minutes) adding remaining water (1/3 cup) a tablespoon at a time to prevent browning. Add green onions and stir fry for 1 minute. Add berries, salt and pepper. Stir just until mixed. Serve hot as a main course.

special_instructions.instruction: To cook reduced quantities: reduce all ingredients proportionately and then increase water in step 1 by 1 cup.

method.instruction: Recipe can be reduced to one-half by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Pour water into cooker. Bring to boil on high heat. Add salt and rice. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 25 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure.
Open cooker. Transfer rice to a colander and drain.
Recipe can be reduced to one-half by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Melt butter in cooker. Add onion and celery. Stir fry till onion is transparent. Add remaining ingredients except sour cream. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 15 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Serve hot. Garnish each individual serving with a teaspoon of sour cream.
In a pan, roast together pomegranate and cumin seeds and grind to a powder.
Put water, chickpeas, 4 tsp salt, cardamoms, cinnamon and cloves in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 18 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.

Open cooker. Drain off cooking liquid and reserve. Add remaining salt ( 3 ½ tsp), pomegranate-cumin, coriander, pepper, garam masala and mango powders. Mix till chickpeas are coated with spices. Sprinkle chillies and ginger on top.
In a pan, heat oil and ghee till smoky ( approximately 5 minutes) and pour evenly over chickpeas. Add cooking liquid.
Place cooker on medium heat. Cook till liquid dries up and oil shows seperately (approximately 10 minutes), stirring occasionally.
Place chickpeas on serving dish. Serve hot, granished with onion and lemon wedges.

name: Cheru Payar Paruppu Pradhaman
translation: Split Green Gram with Coconut Milk - Kerala Style

method.instruction: In a pan, heat 1 tbsp ghee for about 1 minute. Add coconut and fry till light brown. Remove and keep aside.

Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add red chillies, bay leaves, cloves, cinnamon, cardamoms and cumin seeds. Stir for a few seconds. Add coconut and all other ingredients except water and remaining ghee. Stir fry for about 2 minutes. Add water. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 6 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Pour remaining ghee (1 tbsp) evenly over dal. Serve hot.

name: Dahi Imli Kadi
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre

method.instruction: Sift gram flour into a large bowl. Add curd. Mix till smooth. Gradually add water, stirring constantly. Add turmeric and chilli powders, green chillies, ginger, curry leaves, salt and sugar. Stir.

Heat oil in the Bowl on medium-high heat for about 2 minutes. Add fenugreek seeds. Stir for a few seconds. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add cumin seeds. Stir for a few seconds. Step 3 must follow immediately.
Stir curd mixture and pour into the Bowl. Add tamarind pulp, potatoes and coriander leaves. Stirring constantly, bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low. Simmer, stirring occasionally, till kadi thickens and potatoes are cooked (about 1 ½ hours).
To make tempering: Heat ghee in a pan on medium heat for about 1 minute. Remove from heat. Add chilli powder and pour over kadi. Stir. Serve hot with rice.

special_instructions.instruction: When using the 2 Litre Bowl, cook on low or medium heat only.  Cooking on high heat may damage the plastic handles.  For 2 Litre Bowl, reduce the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%).  As this is a smaller vessel than the 3 Litre Bowl, this recipe may cook about 20% faster than the times stated.  Please check after 80% of the stated cooking time to see whether the food is cooked as you would like it and adjust the cooking time as required.
When using the 4 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%) and increase cooking time as required.
When using the 5 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by two-thirds (66%) and increase cooking time as required.
When using the 6 Litre Bowl, you may double the quantity of ingredients (200%) and increase cooking time as required.

method.instruction: Grind together ginger and garlic into a paste.
Pour 5 cups water into cooker. Add Bengal gram, bottle gourd and turmeric powder. Stir once.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker.

In a large pan, heat oil for about 2 minutes. Add chillies and stir for a few seconds. Add asafoetida and ginger-garlic paste. Stir and fry for about 1 minute. Add cooked dal, all other ingredients and remaining water (1 cup). Bring to boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat and simmer for about 5 minutes. Serve hot.
To cook reduced quantities : do not reduce water at all.
Pour water into cooker. Add beans. Bring to boil on high heat. Skim off scum, if any.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on medium heat. Reduce heat and cook for 18 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Drain beans. Remove skins that have come loose.

Heat oil in rinsed cooker for about 2 minutes. Add onion and garlic. Stir fry till onion is transparent. Add tomatoes. Cook for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add beans and remaining ingredients except parsley. Mix well. Reduce heat and simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Serve hot, garnished with parsley.

name: Khasta Kachoris
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: To make dough: Sift together flour and salt into a bowl. Add vanaspati and rub it in with your fingers till mixture resembles fine bread crumbs. Gradually add water, mixing and gathering flour to make a stiff ball. Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 3 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth for ½ hour.

To make filling: Place black gram in a Futura or other heavy frying pan on medium heat. Stir constantly till gram darkens by a few shades to a light brown and gives out a strong aroma (about 4 minutes). Grind roasted gram to a powder.
Grind aniseed, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, cardamom, cloves and cinnamon to a coarse powder.

Heat vanaspati in a pan on medium heat for about 1 minute. Add asafoetida and spice mixture. Stir for a few seconds. Add powdered gram. Stir. Add salt, chilli powder and water. Stir fry for about 2 minutes. Add sugar. Mix. Allow mixture to cool.

To make kachoris: Knead dough briefly. Divide into 15 portions. Roll one portion into a flat round 8 cm in diameter. Place round on palm of hand, pressing centre to form a cup shape. Put 1 teaspoon filling in centre. Press filling down firmly. Gather dough from sides to the centre, pinching together to form a peak. Push down peak, pressing to seal any openings. Pat kachori between palms to flatten to about 8 cm in diameter. Dampen fingers to help sealing only if necessary. Assemble remaining portions in the same way.

Heat vanaspati in deep-fry pan on medium-high heat for about 3 minutes (90ºC). Add 4 kachoris one after the other. Reduce heat to medium-low. As kachoris start to rise in the oil push down each kachori with back of a perforated spoon and hold under oil for 10-15 seconds. Continue this procedure till kachoris are puffed. Fry till golden brown. Remove and drain. Fry remaining kachoris in the same way except do not increase or reduce heat. Serve hot, accompanied with chutney.
Pour water into cooker. Add Bengal gram and 3 teaspoons salt.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 20 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Transfer gram to a colander and drain.

Heat oil in a large pan for about 2 minutes. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add red chillies and black gram. Stir and fry till black gram is golden brown. Add green chillies. Stir for a few seconds. Add cooked gram and remaining salt (2 teaspoons). Stir and fry for about 2 minutes. Add coconut. Mix well. Serve hot.
Pour water into cooker. Bring to boil on high heat. Add all other ingredients except those for tempering. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 50 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Partially mash dal with back of a ladle.
Place cooker on low heat. Simmer to obtain a creamy consistency (approximately 7 minutes), stirring occasionally.

For tempering, heat ghee in a pan for about 1 minute. Add onion and fry till transparent. Add ginger and continue frying till onion is golden brown. Remove pan from heat. Add chilli powder and pour over dal. Stir. Serve hot.

name: Masoor Croquettes
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Put lentils and 2 cups water in Hawkins pressure cooker. Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 6 minutes. Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally. Open cooker. Cook on medium-high heat till liquid has evaporated, stirring constantly.
Soak bread in remaining water (1 cup) for 15 seconds. Squeeze out water. Mix bread, lentils and all other ingredients. Make 12 barrel-shaped croquettes 6 cm long, 2½ cm in diameter.

Heat oil in deep-fry pan on high heat for about 5 minutes (190ºC). Add 2 croquettes one after the other. Reduce heat to medium. Fry till golden brown. Remove and drain. Fry remaining croquettes in the same way, increasing heat after each batch. Serve hot, accompanied with chutney or tomato ketchup.
*Egg may be omitted by adding 1½ tablespoons cornflour and deleting bread.

name: Matimah Khar
translation: Black Gram with Ash Gourd - Assam Style

name: Medu Wadas
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Grind black gram into a fine paste gradually adding water. In a bowl, beat paste with fork for about 5 minutes to fluff up thus: move fork up from bottom of bowl, breaking paste's surface and plunging fork down in a continuous circular motion rapidly.
Add all other ingredients. Mix.

Heat oil in deep-fry pan on high heat for about 5 minutes (190ºC). Reduce heat to medium-high. Wet your left palm. Put about ¼ cup paste on palm. Wet fingertips of right hand. Pat paste into round shape and slightly flatten to about 2½ cm thick. Make a hole in the centre with a finger. Wet right hand fingers. Overturn wada and place on right hand fingers. Put thumb in centre to enlarge hole. Slip wada into hot oil. Repeat this procedure for 2 more wadas. Fry till dark golden brown. Remove and drain. Fry remaining wadas in the same way except do not increase or reduce heat. Serve hot, accompanied with sambar or chutney.

name: Olan
translation: Red Karamani Beans and Pumpkin in Coconut Milk

name: Paruppu Usli
translation: Spicy Red Gram with Cluster Beans

method.instruction: Grind red gram, 3 tsp salt, green and red chillies into a coarse paste.
Put ground paste into a greased 1 litre container. Level the top. Cover container tightly with aluminium foil securely tied.
Pour 1 ½ cups water into cooker. Put grid in cooker. Place container on grid.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 8 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.

Open cooker. Take out and uncover container. Run a knife around edge of container. Place a dish on top of container and invert. Shake gently to release. Remove container. When slightly cool, crumble gram.
Remove grid. Wash cooker.
Put remaining water (½ cup), beans, turmeric powder and remaining salt (1 tsp) in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Press finger-tip control/lift vent weight lightly to release pressure. Open cooker.
Transfer beans to a colander and drain.

In a large pan, heat oil for about 3 minutes. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, add asafoetida and black gram. Stir fry till gram is golden brown. Add beans. Stir fry for about 3 minutes. Add crumbled gram. Stir fry for about 5 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Pinto or Kidney Bean Curry or Rajma

method.instruction: To make dough, put flour in a bowl. Gradually add 2 ¼ cups water and mix, gathering flour to make a ball. Add oil. Knead well to make a soft dough. Cover dough with a damp cloth. Keep aside for about 1 hour.
Put remaining water (4 ½ cups), red gram and sodium bicarbonate in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 4 minutes. Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Mash dal with back of a ladle.
Place cooker on medium heat. Add sugar and cardamom powder. Mix. Continue cooking until mixture thickens and leaves the sides of the cooker (approximately 30 minutes), stirring constantly.
Remove cooker from heat. When cool, divide dal mixture into 45 equal portions.
Divide dough into 45 equal portions and form balls of 4 cm diameter.

On a floured board, roll a ball of dough into a flat round, 8 cm in diameter. Place a portion of dal mixture in the centre. Gather edges of dough and bring up towards centre to enclose dal mixture. Twist slightly and press to close ball. Gently flatten the ball and coat lightly with flour. Using a rolling pin, roll out on a floured board into a round 12 cm in diameter.
Place puran poli on heated griddle and cook for 1 minute, moving it around to ensure even browning. Turn over and similarly cook the other side for 1 minute.
Place puran poli on serving dish. Spread 1 tsp ghee on top. Serve hot.
Repeat the process till all the dal mixture and dough are used in making puran polis.
Grind ginger and garlic into a paste.

Heat oil in cooker for about 3 minutes. Add chillies. Stir for a few seconds. Add onions and fry till golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste. Stir for a few seconds. Add tomatoes, chilli powder and salt. Cook till tomatoes are pulpy and oil shows separately (approximately 5 minutes), stirring occasionally. Add beans and water. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 20 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Partially mash beans with back of a ladle till gravy is creamy. Add garam masala powder. Stir. Serve hot.

name: Rasam
translation: Hot and Sour Soup - Tamil Style

name: Sambar
translation: Red Gram and Vegetable Curry - Tamil Style

method.instruction: Heat oil in a pan for about 1 minute and roast together whole chillies, Bengal gram, asafoetida, coconut, 1 sprig curry leaves, coriander, cumin and fenugreek seeds till coconut turns golden brown.
Grind roasted ingredients into a paste, adding a little water (½ cup) from time to time.
Put 3 cups water, red gram, turmeric powder and salt in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 4 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Mash dal completely with back of a ladle to blend with liquid. Add ground paste, tamarind pulp, onion, drumsticks, remaining curry leaves (1 sprig) and water (2 cups). Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Reduce pressure by placing cooker in a basin of cold water for about 2 minutes. Open cooker.
For tempering, heat oil in a pan for about 1 minute. Add mustard seeds. When crackling, pour over sambar. Stir. Serve hot.

name: Sukhi Dal
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi (Saucepan)

method.instruction: In a bowl, cover gram with water at least 2 cm above gram. Soak for 1 hour. Drain.

Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in Handi on medium heat for about 2 minutes. Add chopped onion and stir fry till transparent (about 1 minute). Add gram, salt, turmeric, chilli and coriander powders. Stir fry for about 3 minutes.
Add tomato and green chillies. Mix. Add water. Stir. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to very low. Cover. Put ¼ cup water on lid. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Scatter sliced onion over dal.
In a small pan, heat remaining ghee (2 tablespoons) on medium heat for about 1 minute. Pour ghee over dal in Handi. Add coriander leaves.
Cover. Add ¼ cup water to water already on lid. Cook for 10 minutes, or till dal is cooked. Serve hot with paratha.
Put 4 cups water, red gram, turmeric powder and salt in cooker. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Mash dal well with back of a ladle to blend with liquid.
Place cooker on high heat.Add remaining water (3 cups) and all other ingredients except those for tempering. Bring to boil. Reduce heat and cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.

For tempering, heat oil in a pan for about 1 minute. Add mustard and cumin seeds. When crackling, add red chillies, curry leaves and asafoetida. Stir for a few seconds and pour over dal. Stir. Serve hot.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan for about 1 minute. Add sliced onions, ginger and garlic. Fry till onions are transparent. Add coconut. Stir fry till coconut is pale brown. Grind fried ingredients, coriander leaves, red chilli and garam masala powders into a paste, adding a little water (½ cup) from time to time.

Heat remaining oil(½ cup) in cooker for about 3 minutes. Add chopped onions and fry till pale brown. Add ground paste. Stir for a few seconds. Add tomatoes, sugar, salt and ¼ cup water. Cook till tomatoes are pulpy and oil shows separately (approximately 5 minutes), stirring occasionally.
Add peas. Mix. Add remaining water (4 cups). Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 12 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Serve hot.

name: Vorn
translation: Split Green Gram with Coconut Milk - Mangalore Style

method.instruction: Beat together curd, milk and condensed milk till well blended. Add almonds, pistachio nuts and raisins. Mix.
Pour the mixture into a 1 litre pudding mold. Cover mold tightly with aluminium foil securely tied.
Pour water into cooker. Put grid in cooker. Place mold on grid.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 20 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Take out and uncover mold. Allow to cool to room temperature. Drain off water if any. Refrigerate until well chilled (approximately 4 hours).
Place a serving dish on top of mold and invert. Shake gently to release. Remove mold. Serve cold.

name: Cheese Toasts Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Sift together flour and cocoa.
Put milk and butter in a saucepan. Bring to simmer on low heat, stirring occasionally.
Stir in prepared flour. Increase heat to medium and cook, stirring constantly, till mixture thickens and leaves the sides of the pan (approximately 2 minutes).
Remove pan from heat. Allow mixture to cool for about 3 minutes. Stir in sugar, egg yolks and vanilla.
Beat egg whites till they form stiff peaks. Fold lightly into mixture.
Pour mixture into a greased 1 litre mould. Cover mould tightly aluminium foil or grease-proof paper securely tied.
Pour water into cooker. Put grid in cooker. Place mould on grid.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 20 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Take out and uncover mould. Serve hot.

To make caramel:  In a small, heavy saucepan, combine ½ cup/100 g sugar and ¼ cup/60 ml water.  Place pan on low heat and stir till sugar is completely dissolved.  After all sugar is dissolved, increase heat to medium. Bring to boil and continue cooking till syrup turns a deep golden colour.  Remove pan from heat.  Wearing oven mitts, pour caramel in a 1 quart / 1 litre mold and tip and turn the mold to coat evenly bottom and sides.
Scald milk and allow to cool slightly.
In a bowl, beat eggs lightly to mix yolks and whites.  Add vanilla and remaining sugar (¼ cup/60 ml).  Stir.  Gradually add milk, stirring constantly.
Pour milk mixture in caramel coated mold.  Cover mold with aluminium foil securely tied.
Pour remaining water (1 cup/240 ml) in cooker.  Place grid in cooker.  Place mold on grid.
Close cooker.  Bring to full pressure on high heat.  Reduce heat and cook 10 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat.  Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker.  Take out and uncover mold.  (Custard will continue to set as it cools).  Allow to cool to room temperature.  Refrigerate.
Place a serving dish (large enough to accommodate caramel sauce) on top of mold and invert.  Shake gently to release.  Remove mold.  Serve cold.

name: Egg Ras Malai
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre

name: Firni
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre

method.instruction: Grind rice, gradually adding water, till smooth.
Bring milk to boil in the Bowl on medium-high heat, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat to medium.
Boil, stirring and scraping the sides occasionally to prevent skin forming on top and milk thickening on sides, till milk is reduced by about 1 cup (about 15 minutes).
Add sugar. Stir till sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat.
Mix ground rice. Gradually add to milk, stirring constantly.
Simmer on low heat for about 20 minutes, stirring constantly.
Pour firni into 6  shallow dessert bowls (about ¾ cup in each). Sprinkle cardamom and pistachio nuts. Allow to cool to room temperature. Refrigerate. Serve cold.

special_instructions.instruction: When using the 2 Litre Bowl, cook on low or medium heat only.  Cooking on high heat may damage the plastic handles.  For 2 Litre Bowl, reduce the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%).  As this is a smaller vessel than the 3 Litre Bowl, this recipe may cook about 20% faster than the times stated.  Please check after 80% of the stated cooking time to see whether the food is cooked as you would like it and adjust the cooking time as required.
When using the 4 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by one-third (33%) and increase cooking time as required.  In Step 6, cook 30 minutes.
When using the 5 Litre Bowl, you may increase the quantity of ingredients by two-thirds (66%) and increase cooking time as required.  In Step 6, cook 30 minutes.
When using the 6 Litre Bowl, you may double the quantity of ingredients (200%).  In Step 6, cook 25 minutes - shorter time due to a broader base and thus faster evaporation of milk.

name: Firni
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick 3 Litre Cook-n-Serve Stewpot

name: Gulab Jamuns
translation: Deep - Fried Milk Balls in Syrup
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: To make paneer: Bring milk to boil in a pan. Add lemon juice, stirring constantly. When milk curdles, remove pan from heat. Place muslin over a bowl. Pour curdled milk into muslin and strain. Gather up corners of muslin and tie about 5 cm above curds. Hang this bag for about 15 minutes. Gently squeeze bag, take down and place between two cutting boards. Place on top board about 2 kg heavy object for about ½ hour. Remove weight. Untie bag. Weigh 150 gm paneer to make gulab jamuns.

To make syrup: Put water, sugar, cardamoms and lemon slices in a pan. Bring to boil on high heat stirring till sugar is dissolved. Reduce heat to medium-high and boil for about 15 minutes. Strain syrup through a muslin cloth.
Sift together flour and baking powder. Knead khoya for 10 minutes. Knead paneer for 5 minutes. Mix khoya, paneer and flour. Knead for 5 minutes.
Divide dough into 36 portions. Put 1 green cardamom seed and 2 grains sugar in the centre of 1 portion. Roll between palms to make a smooth, crack-free ball. Make remaining balls in the same way.

Heat oil in deep-fry pan on high heat for about 2 minutes (90ºC). Reduce heat to low. Add 8 balls one after the other. Fry till dark golden brown (turn balls occasionally by gently shaking the pan instead of using a ladle). Remove and drain. Put gulab jamuns in warm syrup. Fry remaining balls in the same way except do not increase or reduce heat. Keep aside gulab jamuns in syrup for at least 1 hour. Reheat. Serve hot.
*High quality whole milk should yield the 150 gm paneer required for this recipe.

name: Kadai Paneer
cookware_note: 2 Litre Futura Nonstick Deep-Fry Pan (Stir-Fry Pan) with Glass Lid

name: Kadai Paneer
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Deep-Fry Pan 2.5 Litre

method.instruction: Roast together coriander seeds, cumin seeds and peppercorns. Grind roasted spices to a coarse mixture.

Heat oil in Deep-Fry Pan on medium-high heat for about 1 minute. Add onions. Stir fry till onions are translucent (about 7 minutes). Add spice mixture and all other ingredients except paneer and coriander leaves. Stir fry about 1 minute. Add half the quantity of paneer. Mix gently. Add remaining paneer. Mix gently. Cover and cook on medium-low heat (about 4 minutes), stirring gently every two minutes. Uncover pan and cook till liquid has reduced (about 1 minute). Remove pan from heat. Add 2 tbsp/30 ml coriander leaves. Mix. Serve hot, garnished with remaining coriander leaves (2 tbsp/30 ml).

name: Paneer Pakoras
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: To make batter: Sift together gram flour and salt into a bowl. Gradually add water, stirring constantly to make a smooth batter. Add chilli powder. Mix.
To make chutney: Mix lemon juice and water. Grind coriander, mint leaves and green chilli into a paste gradually adding lemon juice mixture. Add salt. Mix.
Spread ¼ teaspoon chutney between two pieces of paneer. Assemble remaining paneer pieces in the same way.

Heat oil in deep-fry pan on high heat for about 5 minutes (180ºC). Dip 5 assembled pieces in batter to coat. Add one after the other to hot oil. Reduce heat to medium-high. Fry till golden brown. Remove and drain. Fry remaining assembled pieces in the same way, increasing heat after each batch. Serve hot.

name: Paneer Pasanda
translation: Cottage Cheese Simmered in Spices and Cream
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 3 Litre Handi (Saucepan)

name: Paneer Tikki
cookware_note: on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Tava (Griddle)

method.instruction: To make soft paneer: Bring milk to boil in a large pan. Add lemon juice, stirring constantly (about 1 minute) till milk has curdled (curd separated from whey). Remove pan from heat. Place muslin cloth over a bowl. Pour curdled milk into muslin and strain. Gather up corners of muslin and tie about 2 inch/5 cm above curds. Hang this bag about 30 minutes. Gently squeeze bag to drain excess whey. Untie bag. Weigh 14 oz/400 g paneer to make tikki. Gently crumble paneer.
Immerse each bread slice in water for 15 seconds. Squeeze out and discard water. Break bread into tiny pieces.
Mix gently with fingertips paneer, bread and all other ingredients except oil. Make 20 slightly rounded patties about 2 inch/5 cm in diameter,  ½ inch/1.3 cm thick.
Spread 1 tsp/5 ml oil on tava all over the area where the patties will be placed. Place on medium heat about 1minute for 22 cm tava/about 2 minutes for 26 cm tava. Reduce heat to low.
Add 4 patties. Fry on both sides till golden brown (about 3 minutes Add 4 patties. Fry on both sides till golden brown (about 3 minutes on each side). Remove.
Fry remaining patties in the same way as in step 5, adding 1 tsp/ 5 ml oil for each batch. Serve hot, accompanied with chutney or tomato ketchup

name: Paneer Tikki
cookware_note: on Futura Nonstick Tava (Griddle) 26 cm

name: Rabri
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

method.instruction: Bring milk to boil in deep-fry pan on high heat, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat to low. As a layer of cream forms on top, move layer to the edges, slightly pushing it up the sides of pan and leaving it to set. Continue this process till milk is reduced to about 1 cup.
Add sugar. Stir till sugar is dissolved. Remove pan from heat. Scrape cream from the sides into milk. Mix.
Place rabri in serving bowl. Sprinkle almonds and pistachio nuts. Serve at room temperature or cold.
Heat ½ cup ghee in cooker for about 2 minutes. Add vermicelli. Stir fry till golden brown (approximately 3 minutes). Add milk and sugar. Stir till sugar is dissolved.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on medium heat. Reduce heat and cook for 1 minute.
Remove cooker from heat. Reduce pressure by placing cooker in a basin of cold water for about 2 minutes.
Open cooker. Add cardamom powder.
Place cooker on high heat. Cook till payasam is slightly thickened (approximately 5 minutes), stirring constantly.
In a pan, heat remaining ghee (¼ cup) for about 2 minutes. Add cashew nuts. Stir fry till golden brown. Add raisins. Stir for a few seconds and pour over payasam. Stir. Serve hot.

name: Shahi Tukra
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised 2.5 Litre Deep-Fry Pan

name: Akuri
cookware_note: Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: In a bowl, beat eggs just till yolks and whites are mixed.  Add milk, salt and pepper.  Mix.
Heat oil in frying pan on medium heat about 3 minutes (for pan other than 26 cm see

special_instructions.instruction: below).  Add onion and ginger.  Stir fry till onion is transparent (about 2 minutes).  Add tomato, chillies and turmeric.  Stir fry about 1 minute.  Reduce heat to low.  Add egg mixture and coriander leaves.  When mixture starts to set on bottom and sides, stir and lift so uncooked part goes to bottom.  Continue cooking till eggs have just thickened and are moist and soft, stirring constantly.  Sprinkle garam masala powder on top. Serve hot, accompanied with toast, phulka or paratha.
For 18 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Reduce ingredients by one-half.  Step 2:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 22 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Reduce ingredients by one-third.  Step 2:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 26 cm pan (rounded sides) ingredients may be increased by one-half.
For 30 cm pan, increase ingredients by two-thirds.  Step 2:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 3 minutes.

name: Akuri
cookware_note: Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm

name: Country Omelette Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: In a pan, boil water and salt.  Add peas, potato and onion.  Cover and cook on low heat till vegetables are just tender (about 7 minutes).  Drain and discard water.
In a bowl, beat eggs with a fork just till yolks and whites are mixed.  Add vegetables and chillies.  Mix.
Heat frying pan on medium heat about 3 minutes (for pan other than 26 cm see

special_instructions.instruction: below). Add butter and spread over inside base with wooden spatula.  Heat till butter has foamed up and subsides.  Quickly stir egg mixture and pour into pan.  When sides start setting (this should happen immediately), lift an edge of omelette with spatula and tilt pan so unset egg runs under edge.  Quickly continue lifting and tilting procedure all around omelette till egg no longer runs freely.  Loosen edges with spatula and shake pan gently so omelette slides in one piece.  Slide omelette onto a large plate. Wearing oven mitts, place upside down pan over omelette and invert plate on pan, drop Note:  Omelette may be made with 1 cup of any diced, cooked vegetables, chicken, ham, seafood etc.
For 18 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Reduce ingredients by one-half.  Step 3:  Heat pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 22 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Reduce ingredients by one-half.  Step 3:  Heat pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 30 cm pan, increase ingredients by one-half.  Step 3: Heat pan on medium heat about 3 minutes.

name: Egg Curry Futura Hard Anodised Cook-n-Serve Bowl 3 Litre
Egg Curry 3 Litre Futura Nonstick Saucepan with Glass Lid

method.instruction: To boil eggs: Place eggs in pan and cover with water at least 1 inch/2.5 cm above eggs. Bring to boil on medium-high heat. Reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer 15 minutes. Meanwhile, do step 2. Pour off water. Pour tap water slowly over eggs till eggs are cool. Drain. Remove and keep aside eggs. Wash and dry pan.
Grind into a paste ginger, garlic and green chilli.
Heat ¼ cup/60 ml oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes. Add onions. Fry till light golden brown. Add cumin seeds and ginger paste. Stir a few seconds. Add tomatoes. Mix. Remove from heat.
In a mixer-blender/grinder, mix tomato mixture till smooth. Wash and dry pan.

Heat remaining oil (1 tbsp/15 ml) in pan on medium heat about 1 minute. Add cloves, cardamoms and cinnamon. Stir a few seconds. Reduce heat to low. Add tomato mixture and remaining ingredients except eggs and water. Cook about 3 minutes, stirring constantly. Add water. Stir. Bring to boil on medium-high heat. Reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer (about 20 minutes). Meanwhile, shell eggs and cut lengthwise into halves. When gravy is slightly thickened, remove pan from heat.
Add eggs, keeping yolk sides up. Cover and simmer on low heat just till eggs are hot (about 2 minutes). Serve hot.

name: Egg Curry Futura Nonstick 3 Litre Saucepan
Egg Rolls on 33 cm Futura Nonstick Dosa Tava
cooking_time: , mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes very wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.

method.instruction: Mix flour, salt, sugar and milk. Add 2 tbsp + 2 tsp/40 ml water. Mix. Add enough of remaining water (2 tbsp/30 ml), 1 tbsp/15 ml at a time, mixing after each addition, till dough forms a soft ball (stop adding water before dough becomes very wet and sticky). Knead till dough is smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes). Keep covered with a damp cloth about 30 minutes.
Rub hands with a few drops of oil. Knead dough briefly. Make 4 balls about 1½ inch/3.8 cm in diameter. Keep covered with a damp cloth.
On a floured board, roll a ball of dough into a flat round 8 inch/20 cm in diameter. Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil all over the top surface of the round.

Starting at one edge, tightly roll up round (Figure J). With palms of both hands roll back and forth till extended to 12 inch/30 cm long (Figure K). Wind around one end into a flat coil (Figure L). Put free end over centre top of coil (Figure M). Press down free end gently. Flatten coil slightly.
Make remaining balls into coils in the same way as in step 3 and step 4. Keep covered with a damp cloth.
On a floured board, roll each coil into a flat round 8 inch/20 cm in diameter. Keep on a lightly floured surface, covered with a damp cloth.

Break 2 eggs into each of 4 small bowls. Beat eggs in each bowl with a fork just till yolks and whites are mixed. Keep aside. Put 1/8 tsp/0.6 ml salt and 1/8 tsp/0.6 ml pepper onto each of 4 small plates to make four portions. Keep aside.

Heat tava on medium heat no more than 5 minutes. Put one round on tava. Cook 1 minute (Top should begin to look dry. There should be a few light golden brown specks on the underside - if the round does not have the desired light golden brown specks on the underside, cook 30 seconds longer). Turn over. Spread  ¼ tsp/1.3 ml oil all over the top surface of round (about 15 seconds). Cook about 45 seconds or till a few light golden brown specks appear. Turn over. Spread  ¼ tsp/1.3 ml oil all over the top surface of round (about 15 seconds). Lightly press entire round with spatula, rotating and pressing a small area at a time (about 45 seconds). Turn over. Cook, pressing round in the same way, about 1 minute. (Roti should be evenly cooked on both sides, with golden brown spots. It should not be very crisp so do not allow spots to become dark brown. A roti takes about 4 minutes to cook.) Remove. Keep aside. Cook remaining rounds in the same way. Wipe tava with a kitchen cloth or paper napkin.

Spread ½ tsp/2.5 ml oil outwards from centre of tava over the circular area (about 8 inch/20 cm in diameter) where the eggs will be poured. Quickly take one of the bowls containing eggs, add one portion salt and pepper (pre-measured in step 7), beat till mixed and pour egg mixture in centre of tava. Quickly place one roti over egg mixture. Press top surface of roti lightly with spatula till eggs stick to roti (about 30 seconds). Cook till eggs are set (about 30 seconds). Turn over. Cook about 30 seconds. Slide roti (egg side up) onto a plate. Put empty, hot tava on a coaster or grid. Do step 10 without delay.

Scatter ¼ cup/25 g onion rings over eggs. Sprinkle ¼ tsp/1.3 ml green chilli (optional), ½ tsp/2.5 ml vinegar with green chillies and ½ tsp/2.5 ml chaat masala over onions. Starting at one edge, roll up roti. Serve hot. Quickly return tava to medium heat.
Make and serve remaining rolls in the same way as in step 9 and step 10.
Serving Suggestions
Serve with tomato ketchup or mustard sauce, if desired.
Wrap a piece of aluminium foil around half of roll for holding

name: French Toast Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm
French Toast on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Flat Tava

method.instruction: In a bowl, beat eggs just till yolks and whites are mixed. Add milk, sugar, vanilla essence and salt. Mix.
Turn 1 slice bread in egg mixture leaving each side in mixture about 20 seconds. Remove with a spatula and put on a plate. Turn and remove 1 more slice in the same way.
Heat tava on medium heat about 3 minutes. Spread 1 tsp/5 ml butter on tava all over the area where the slices will be placed.

Add the 2 soaked slices. Pour egg mixture leftover on the plate back into the bowl. Fry on both sides till browned (2-3 minutes on each side). Remove. (While slices are frying turn 2 more slices in egg mixture as described in step 2 and keep aside.)

Soak and fry remaining slices in the same way as in step 4, adding 1 tsp/5 ml butter on tava for each batch. Adjust heat to medium-low if tava is too hot for later batches. Serve hot with honey or jam.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm Flat Tava (Griddle):  Step 3: Each batch: ½ tsp/2.5 ml butter. Step 4: Add 1 soaked slice.

name: Fried Eggs on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Flat Tava
Fried Eggs Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm

method.instruction: Break eggs on a saucer.

Heat frying pan on low heat about 2 minutes. Add butter and spread till melted to cover inside base of pan. Slide eggs into pan. Pour 1 tsp/ 5 ml water over each egg.  Cover and cook about 1 minute or to desired doneness. Remove. Sprinkle salt and pepper. Serve hot.
Variations:
For firmer whites: cover at once but do not add water and proceed as given above; or do not cover frying pan at all.
If eggs are to be turned, you may or may not cover frying pan. Turn eggs after whites have set and yolks are as firm as desired using a thin, broad spatula.

For well done eggs in oil: heat 1 tbsp/15 ml oil on low heat about 5 minutes. Slide in eggs. When whites are set, increase heat to medium; turn eggs or spoon oil over eggs and cook to desired doneness.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm Frying Pan: No change required.
For 22 cm Frying Pan (Rounded Sides): No change required.
For 30 cm Frying Pan, Double all ingredients. Step 2: Heat pan about 3 minutes. Increase heat to mediumlow after pouring 1 tsp/5 ml water on each egg).

method.instruction: Break 2 eggs on a saucer.  Repeat with another saucer.

Heat tava on medium heat about 4 minutes (for preheating tava other than 30 cm,  see special instructions).  Add butter and spread on tava with wooden spatula.  Slide eggs onto tava.  Cook about 1 minute or to desired doneness.  Sprinkle salt and pepper.  Serve hot.

special_instructions.instruction: For 22 cm tava, reduce ingredients by one-half; cook 2 eggs at a time.    Step 2:  Heat tava on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 26 cm tava, in Step 2, heat tava on medium heat about 3 minutes.

name: Omelette - Indian Style Futura Hard Anodised Frying Pan 25 cm
Omelette – Indian Style Futura Nonstick Frying Pan 26 cm

method.instruction: Mix onion, tomato, coriander leaves and chilli.
Separate egg yolks and whites.  Beat egg whites till frothy.  Beat egg yolks and salt lightly.  Add yolks to whites.  Mix gently.
Heat oil in frying pan on medium heat about 3 minutes (for pan other than 26 cm see

special_instructions.instruction: below).  Spread oil over inside base with wooden spatula.  Add eggs.  If necessary, gently spread eggs to cover base.  Cook till edges start to set.  Scatter onion mixture over eggs.  Cook till underside is lightly browned (about 1½ minutes).  Loosen omelette edges with spatula.  Using spatula, lift half omelette and fold over other half.  Cook about 30 seconds.  Turn over.  Cook about 30 seconds.  Serve hot.
For 18 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Reduce ingredients by one-half.  Step 3:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 22 cm pan, a small burner is recommended.  Step 3:  Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 2 minutes.
For 30 cm pan, increase ingredients by one-half.  Step 3: Heat oil in pan on medium heat about 3 minutes.

name: Pancakes on 26 cm Futura Hard Anodised Flat Tava

method.instruction: To cook reduced quantities : do not reduce water at all.
Pour water into cooker. Put grid in cooker. Place chicken on grid, putting in remaining ingredients among chicken pieces.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Remove chicken. Discard vegetables and peppercorns.
This chicken can be used in salads, casseroles, soups, pies, etc. Allow the chicken to cool, pull it from the bones and shred, chop or dice as desired.
Recipe can be reduced to one-third by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Rub chicken with salt and pepper.
Group chicken into 3 batches. Heat oil in cooker for about 3 minutes. Brown and remove each batch.
Away from heat, sprinkle water in cooker. Stir to remove any frying residues attached to base.
Return cooker to heat. Add onions and garlic. Stir fry for about 2 minutes. Add chicken and remaining ingredients except ½ cup ketchup and parsley. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 6 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Add remaining ketchup (½ cup). Stir. Serve hot with noodles, garnished with parsley.

name: Chicken Biryani
cookware_note: 3L Cook-n-Serve Stewpot

method.instruction: Put all ingredients except capsicum and lady's fingers in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 6 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Take out chicken pieces. Discard bones. Cut chicken into small cubes. Put chicken cubes, capsicum and lady's fingers in cooker.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Remove cooker immediately from heat. Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Serve hot.
Recipe can be reduced to one-third by reducing all ingredients proportionately.
Group chicken into 3 batches. Heat oil in cooker for about 2 minutes. Brown and remove each batch. Drain off oil, leaving 3 tbsp in cooker.
Away from heat, sprinkle water in cooker. Stir to remove any frying residues attached to base.
Return cooker to heat. Add onion, mushrooms, boiling onions and garlic. Stir fry till onion is transparent. Add chicken, ½ cup wine and remaining ingredients except olives and cognac. Stir.
Close cooker. Bring to full pressure on high heat. Reduce heat and cook for 6 minutes.
Remove cooker from heat.  Allow to cool naturally.
Open cooker. Place chicken on serving dish. Keep hot.

Add remaining wine (½ cup), olives, and cognac to the cooking liquid in cooker. Return cooker to medium heat and boil till liquid is reduced to half, stirring occasionally. Pour over chicken. Serve hot.
